methods_all.name: Discounting biased equity research
methods_all.description: Portfolio managers and investors should discount or ignore equity research that creates potential for bias.

methods_all.name: Parsimony Principle
methods_all.description: Adhere to using only necessary inputs in valuation models.

methods_all.name: Skepticism towards market efficiency
methods_all.description: Approach the issue of market efficiency with skepticism, recognizing that markets make mistakes but finding these requires skill and luck.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Models
methods_all.description: A method to estimate the value of an investment based on its expected future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present Value = ∑(Cash Flow_t / (1 + r)^t)

methods_all.name: valuation for support and resistance lines
methods_all.description: Use valuation metrics to determine price levels where buying or selling pressure is expected.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Not specified in text, but involves technical analysis principles.

methods_all.name: information trading
methods_all.description: Trade based on anticipated market reactions to new information.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Analyze the relationship between information and stock price changes.

methods_all.name: Synergy valuation
methods_all.description: Quantitative assessment of synergies in mergers and acquisitions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Consider the combined value of both firms, including potential cost savings and revenue enhancements.

methods_all.name: valuation model application
methods_all.description: Using a valuation model to determine stock value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare the output of the valuation model with market price to identify potential inefficiencies or errors.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation
methods_all.description: relates the value of an asset to the present value of expected future cash flows on that asset.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(CFt / (1+r)^t), where CFt is the cash flow in period t, r is the discount rate, and n is the life of the asset.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Valuation
methods_all.description: Estimating the intrinsic value of an asset based on expected future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t), where CFt is the cash flow in period t, and r is the discount rate reflecting riskiness.

methods_all.name: Equity Valuation
methods_all.description: Discounting expected cash flows to equity at the cost of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = ∑(CF to equity_t / (1 + k_e)^t) for t=1 to n

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A special case of equity valuation focusing on dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = PV(Expected Future Dividends)

methods_all.name: Firm Valuation
methods_all.description: Discounting expected cash flows to the firm at WACC.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Firm = ∑(CF to firm_t / (1 + WACC)^t) for t=1 to n

methods_all.name: Discount Cash Flows to Equity at Cost of Equity
methods_all.description: Calculate the value of equity by discounting cash flows to equity using the cost of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = CFt / (1 + rE)^t, where rE is the cost of equity

methods_all.name: Discount Cash Flows to Firm at Cost of Capital
methods_all.description: Calculate the value of the firm by discounting cash flows to the firm using the weighted average cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Firm = CFt / (1 + WACC)^t, where WACC is the weighted average cost of capital

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Approach
methods_all.description: Calculates the cost of capital for a firm using equity and debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of capital = Cost of equity(Proportion of equity used to fund business) + Pretax cost of debt (1 – Tax rate)(Proportion of debt used to fund business)

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) Approach
methods_all.description: Separates the effects on value of debt financing from the value of the assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of business = Value of business with 100% equity financing + Present value of expected tax benefits of debt - Expected bankruptcy costs

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of asset = Present value of excess return + Investment in the asset

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Reflects value of all assets producing cash flows.

methods_all.name: Relative Valuation
methods_all.description: Uses multiples of comparable firms to estimate value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Comparable Firm's Multiple * Relevant Variable (e.g., earnings, cash flows) for the firm being valued

methods_all.name: Relative Valuation Models
methods_all.description: Comparing multiples of a company to its peers or historical levels.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare P/E, P/S, and other ratios with sector averages or past performance.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Valuation
methods_all.description: Estimating the intrinsic value of an asset based on expected future cash flows discounted at a rate reflecting risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t), where CFt is the cash flow in period t, and r is the discount rate.

methods_all.name: Cross-Sectional Comparisons
methods_all.description: Comparing price-earnings ratio of a firm to the average P/E ratio of other firms in the same industry.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P/E ratio comparison

methods_all.name: Comparisons across Time
methods_all.description: Comparing current multiples with historical multiples for mature firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Historical multiple comparison

methods_all.name: liquidation value
methods_all.description: Aggregate estimated sale proceeds of assets.

methods_all.name: replacement cost
methods_all.description: Estimate cost to replace all firm's assets today.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: A model for valuing options based on underlying asset's current value, variance, strike price, time to expiration, and riskless interest rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C = S*N(d1) - X*e^(-rT)*N(d2)

methods_all.name: Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Used to value assets with embedded options, such as undeveloped natural resource reserves.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Incorporates the right of the asset holder to develop or not based on market conditions.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Estimate value by discounting expected cash flows at a risk-adjusted rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t)

methods_all.name: Relative Valuation
methods_all.description: Determine asset value based on similar assets' pricing.

methods_all.name: Contingent Claim Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing an asset with option-like characteristics using option pricing models.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis
methods_all.description: Calculating firm and equity values using projected cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = Σ(Annual Cash Flows to Equity / (1 + Cost of Equity)^t); Value of Firm = Σ(Annual Cash Flows to the Firm / (1 + Cost of Capital)^t)

methods_all.name: Book Value Principle
methods_all.description: Starts with historical cost as the best estimate of value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unless a substantial reason is given, book value equals historical cost.

methods_all.name: Market Value Skepticism
methods_all.description: Views market or estimated values with suspicion due to volatility and manipulation risks.

methods_all.name: Depreciation Methods
methods_all.description: Straight line and accelerated depreciation methods for fixed assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Straight-line: constant annual depreciation; Accelerated: higher depreciation in early years, lower later.

methods_all.name: Inventory Valuation Methods
methods_all.description: GAAP allows FIFO, LIFO, and weighted average methods.

methods_all.name: First in, first out (FIFO)
methods_all.description: Cost of goods sold is based on the earliest material costs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: COGS = cost of earliest materials; Inventory value approximates current replacement cost.

methods_all.name: Last in, first out (LIFO)
methods_all.description: Cost of goods sold is based on the most recent material costs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: COGS = cost of latest materials; Inventory value reflects earlier purchase prices.

methods_all.name: Weighted average
methods_all.description: Both inventory and COGS are based on the average cost of all materials purchased during the period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Average cost per unit = total cost of units / number of units; COGS and inventory values reflect this average.

methods_all.name: LIFO vs FIFO
methods_all.description: Inventory valuation methods with tax implications.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Firms use LIFO for tax benefits in high inflation periods, increasing cash flows and reducing taxable income.

methods_all.name: Equity Approach
methods_all.description: Used for minority active investments where ownership is between 20% and 50%. Adjusts acquisition cost based on net income, losses, and dividends received.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Proportional share of net income/losses + dividends received adjust the acquisition cost.

methods_all.name: Consolidation Method
methods_all.description: Used for majority active investments where ownership is more than 50%. Replaces financial investment with assets and liabilities of the firm, leading to balance sheet consolidation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: 100% of subsidiary's revenues, earnings, and assets are consolidated into parent's financial statements; minority interest shown as liability.

methods_all.name: Straight-line Amortization
methods_all.description: A method to amortize intangible assets over their expected lives.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Amortization expense = Total cost of intangible asset / Expected useful life

methods_all.name: Goodwill Amortization
methods_all.description: Boeing amortizes goodwill from the Rockwell acquisition over 30 years.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Amortization period = 30 years

methods_all.name: Customer Financing and Accounts Receivable
methods_all.description: Boeing shows present value of future payments as customer financing, current portion as accounts receivable.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of future payments classified as customer financing

methods_all.name: Historical Cost Adjustments
methods_all.description: Accounting method for valuing liabilities and equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use historical costs with adjustments, not expected future cash flows or market value.

methods_all.name: Historical Cost Accounting
methods_all.description: Uses historical costs with adjustments for valuation of assets, liabilities and equities.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Historical Cost + Adjustments

methods_all.name: Lease Accounting
methods_all.description: Determines whether a lease is an operating or capital lease based on specific criteria.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: A lease must be treated as a capital lease if it meets any one of the following conditions: 1) The lease life exceeds 75 percent of the life of the asset; 2) There is a transfer of ownership to the lessee at the end of the lease term; 3) There is an option to purchase the asset at a bargain price at the end of the lease term; 4) The present value of the lease payments, discounted at an appropriate discount rate, exceeds 90 percent of the fair market value of the asset.

methods_all.name: Lease Classification
methods_all.description: Determine if a lease is capital or operating based on present value criteria.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of lease payments exceeds 90% of fair market value for capital leases.

methods_all.name: Pension Funding
methods_all.description: Estimate and fund pension obligations accurately to avoid underfunding liabilities.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Defined benefit plans require estimating future benefits based on contributions, returns, and expected rates of return.

methods_all.name: Deferred Taxes Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates deferred taxes based on difference between reported and actual tax paid.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Deferred Tax Liability = Reported Tax - Actual Tax Paid

methods_all.name: Treasury Stock Method
methods_all.description: Companies can show repurchased stock as treasury stock to reduce book value of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Repurchased stock must be reissued or used for option exercises within a short period; otherwise, it reduces the book value of equity.

methods_all.name: Accrual Accounting
methods_all.description: Recognizes revenue and expenses in the period they are incurred, not when cash transactions occur.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Revenue = Sales; Expenses = Costs associated with generating sales

methods_all.name: Accrual Accounting
methods_all.description: Recognizes revenues and expenses when they are incurred rather than when cash transactions occur.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Revenue is recognized when service has been performed in full or substantially, and firm receives observable and measurable payment. Expenses linked to revenue production are recognized in the same period as revenue.

methods_all.name: Return on Assets (ROA)
methods_all.description: Measures operating efficiency in generating profits from assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Earnings before interest and taxes(1 – Tax rate)/Total assets or [Net income + Interest expenses(1 – Tax rate)]/Total assets

methods_all.name: Pretax ROA
methods_all.description: Measures profitability before tax adjustments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Pretax ROA = Earnings before interest and taxes/Total assets

methods_all.name: Return on Invested Capital (ROIC)
methods_all.description: Evaluates return relative to the cost of capital, using book value of debt and equity net of cash as denominator.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ROC or ROIC = Operating income / Invested capital

methods_all.name: Return on Capital (ROC) Maximization
methods_all.description: Calculates the optimal combination of profit margin and capital turnover to maximize ROC.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ROC = Profit Margin x Capital Turnover

methods_all.name: Return on Capital (ROC)
methods_all.description: Measures profitability of overall firm
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ROC = EBIT(1 – t)/(BV of debt + BV of equity) where EBIT is earnings before interest and taxes, t is tax rate

methods_all.name: Return on Equity (ROE)
methods_all.description: Examines profitability from the perspective of the equity investor
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ROE = Net Income / Book Value of Equity

methods_all.name: Return on Equity Decomposition
methods_all.description: Estimates return on equity by breaking down into after-tax return on capital, debt-equity ratio, and book interest rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Return on Equity = After-Tax Return on Capital + (Debt-Equity Ratio * Book Interest Rate(1 - Tax Rate))

methods_all.name: Current Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures short-term liquidity risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Current assets / Current liabilities

methods_all.name: Quick or Acid Test Ratio
methods_all.description: A variant of the current ratio that excludes inventory and accounts receivable from current assets to measure liquidity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Cash + Marketable Securities) / Current Liabilities

methods_all.name: Turnover Ratios
methods_all.description: Measure efficiency in working capital management by assessing how quickly a company converts its assets into sales or cash.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Accounts Receivable Turnover = Sales / Average Accounts Receivable; Inventory Turnover = Cost of Goods Sold / Average Inventory

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures the firm's capacity to meet interest payments from pre-debt, pretax earnings.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Higher ratio indicates better security in making interest payments.

methods_all.name: Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Extends the denominator of the interest coverage ratio to include other fixed obligations such as lease payments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses EBITDA in numerator and cash fixed charges in denominator.

methods_all.name: Capital Expenditure Ratio
methods_all.description: Captures extent of capital expenditures relative to operating cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Defined by measuring the cash flows from continuing operations, before interest but after taxes and after meeting working capital needs.

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures a company's ability to pay interest expenses with its earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT).
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Interest Coverage Ratio = EBIT / Interest Expense

methods_all.name: Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Assesses a company’s ability to meet fixed charges, including interest expense and lease payments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio = (EBIT + Fixed Charges) / (Interest Expense + Fixed Charges)

methods_all.name: Cash Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Evaluates the cash flow adequacy of a company's ability to cover fixed charges.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cash Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio = Cash Flows from Operations / Cash Fixed Charges

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures a firm's ability to meet interest payments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Interest Coverage Ratio = Operating Income / Interest Expense

methods_all.name: Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Evaluates the capacity of a firm to cover fixed charges including interest and lease payments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Fixed Charges Coverage Ratio = (Operating Income + Fixed Charges Before Tax) / (Interest Expense + Fixed Charges After Tax)

methods_all.name: Debt to Total Capital Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures debt as a proportion of the total capital of the firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Debt to Total Capital Ratio = Debt / (Debt + Equity)

methods_all.name: Debt to Book Value of Equity Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures debt as a proportion of the book value of equity in the firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Debt to Book Value of Equity Ratio = Debt / Book Value of Equity

methods_all.name: Debt Ratio Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the ratio of preferred stock to capital including debt, equity, and preferred stock.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Preferred Stock / (Debt + Equity + Preferred Stock)

methods_all.name: Long-term Debt Ratio
methods_all.description: Uses only long-term debt in the calculation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Long-term Debt / (Debt + Equity + Preferred Stock)

methods_all.name: Market Value Debt Ratio
methods_all.description: Uses market value of debt and equity instead of book values.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (MV of Long-term Debt + MV of Short-term Debt) / (MV of Equity + MV of Preferred Stock)

methods_all.name: Accrual Accounting
methods_all.description: Measures revenues and expenses in the period of transaction occurrence.

methods_all.name: Depreciation and Amortization
methods_all.description: Spreads capital expenditures over multiple periods.

methods_all.name: Net Working Capital Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the net working capital and noncash working capital for Coca-Cola.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Current Assets - Current Liabilities) - (Noncash Current Assets - Noncash Current Liabilities)

methods_all.name: Capitalization of Advertising Expenses
methods_all.description: Determining whether advertising expenses should be treated as operating or capital expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use the capitalization of R&D as a guide.

methods_all.name: Return on Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating return on equity using beginning book value of equity and average book value of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (a) Book Value of Equity at End of Year - Shares Repurchased * Price per Share + Net Income - Dividends = New Book Value of Equity; (b) Return on Equity = Net Income / Beginning Book Value of Equity; (c) Average ROE = Net Income / ((Beginning Book Value + Ending Book Value) / 2)

methods_all.name: Current Ratio Adjustment
methods_all.description: Determines the effect of equal increases in current assets and liabilities on the current ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If the initial current ratio is greater than 1, an equal increase in both will decrease it. If less than 1, such an increase will raise it.

methods_all.name: Risk-Opportunity Trade-off
methods_all.description: Measuring danger and converting it into opportunity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Risk = Danger; Opportunity = Expected Return

methods_all.name: Expected Return Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating expected return on an investment.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Sum of (Probability of Outcome * Return for that Outcome)

methods_all.name: Expected Return and Variance Analysis
methods_all.description: Measuring investment opportunity and danger with expected return and standard deviation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Investors choose between two investments based on higher expected returns if the standard deviations are equal.

methods_all.name: Skewness and Kurtosis Consideration
methods_all.description: Evaluating investment risk beyond mean and variance by considering skewness and kurtosis.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Positive skewness and lower kurtosis distributions preferred over negative skewness and higher kurtosis.

methods_all.name: Historical Risk Assessment
methods_all.description: Evaluating risk based on historical data.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Standard deviations and variances of stocks in various sectors are used to measure risk.

methods_all.name: Diversification
methods_all.description: Investing in multiple assets to reduce firm-specific risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Portfolio variance = σ²A + σ²B + 2ρABσAσB

methods_all.name: Mean-Variance Models
methods_all.description: Measures market risk by considering the distribution of actual returns around expected return.

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: A model that measures nondiversifiable or market risk.

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: A model that describes the relationship between systematic risk and expected return for assets, particularly stocks.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: A model used to determine the expected return of an asset based on its beta and the market's expected return.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(Ri) = Rf + βi(E(Rm) - Rf)

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return on an asset based on beta and market conditions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(Ri) = Rf + βi(E(Rm) - Rf)

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Theory
methods_all.description: A model that breaks down risk into firm-specific and market components.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: R = E(R) + βj * Fj + ε

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Theory (APT)
methods_all.description: A theory that describes the relationship between expected return and risk for assets based on multiple factors.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(Rj) = Rf + β1(E(R1)-Rf) + β2(E(R2)-Rf) + ... + βk(E(Rk)-Rf)

methods_all.name: Multifactor Model
methods_all.description: Determines factors from historical data and correlates them with macroeconomic variables.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(R_GNP) = β_GNP * E(MRP_GNP), E(R_I) = β_I * E(MRP_I)

methods_all.name: Power Law Distribution
methods_all.description: A distribution where the relationship between two variables, Y and X, is described by a constant (α) and a power law exponent (k).
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Y = αX^(-k)

methods_all.name: Power Law Exponents
methods_all.description: A method to measure risk of large movements in assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Assets with higher exponents are considered less risky due to fewer extreme values.

methods_all.name: Jump Process Models
methods_all.description: Models incorporating stock price jumps using Poisson distribution.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Parameters include expected jump size (μ), variance in jump size (δ^2), and frequency of jumps (λ).

methods_all.name: Regression or Proxy Models
methods_all.description: Models that use observable market variables to explain returns, rather than economic theory assumptions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Example: Fama-French model uses firm's market capitalization and price-to-book ratio as explanatory factors for stock returns.

methods_all.name: Fama-French Proxy Model
methods_all.description: Regression model that uses market capitalization and price-to-book ratio to predict stock returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected monthly return = α + β1(Size) + β2(Book-to-Market Ratio)

methods_all.name: Data mining
methods_all.description: Identifying variables related to returns from large datasets.

methods_all.name: Standard error calculation
methods_all.description: Evaluating the precision of historical premia in proxy models.

methods_all.name: Fama-French three-factor model
methods_all.description: Includes size and book-to-market betas to explain returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected return = Risk-free rate + Beta * Market risk premium + Size factor + Book-to-market factor

methods_all.name: CAPM
methods_all.description: Capital Asset Pricing Model for estimating expected returns based on beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(Ri) = Rf + βi(E(Rm) - Rf)

methods_all.name: Cash Flow Coverage
methods_all.description: Measures the magnitude of cash flows relative to obligations.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: CF / Obligations

methods_all.name: Financial Ratios for Default Risk
methods_all.description: Use financial ratios to measure default risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EBITDA/Revenues, ROIC = EBIT/(BV of debt + BV of equity – Cash), Interest coverage ratio (EBIT/Interest expenses), EBITDA/Interest

methods_all.name: ROIC
methods_all.description: Return on Invested Capital calculation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EBIT/(BV of debt + BV of equity – Cash)

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures a company's ability to pay interest expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EBIT/Interest expenses

methods_all.name: Debt-to-EBITDA ratio
methods_all.description: Indicates the extent of financial leverage and debt repayment capacity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: BV of Debt/EBITDA

methods_all.name: Average Annual Return Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate the average annual return based on price changes and dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Ending Value + Dividends - Beginning Value) / Beginning Value

methods_all.name: Standard Deviation and Variance Calculation
methods_all.description: Compute standard deviation and variance of annual returns to measure volatility.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Variance = Σ(Annual Return - Average Annual Return)^2 / n, Standard Deviation = √Variance

methods_all.name: Portfolio variance calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the variance of a portfolio given individual asset returns, standard deviations, and correlations.

methods_all.name: Portfolio Variance Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the variance of a portfolio given number of securities and covariance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Variance = Covariance * (1 - (n-1)/N) where n is the number of securities, N approaches infinity.

methods_all.name: Expected Return and Standard Deviation Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return and standard deviation after shifting funds between risky assets and risk-free asset.

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Determines the proportion of wealth invested in market portfolio and riskless asset based on desired standard deviation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: σp = wM*σM, E(Rp) = wM*E(Rm) + (1-wM)*Rf

methods_all.name: Covariance and Variance Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimates covariance between two assets and their individual variances.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: cov(X,Y) = Σ[(Xt - Xavg)*(Yt - Yavg)]/n, Var(X) = Σ(Xt - Xavg)^2/n

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimates the beta of an asset using market returns and covariance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: β = cov(Ri,Rm)/Var(Rm)

methods_all.name: Correlation Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates correlation between two assets given their standard deviations and covariance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ρ(X,Y) = cov(X,Y)/(σX*σY)

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Estimates expected return using factor betas and risk premiums.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: E(Ri) = Rf + β1*(Rm1-Rf) + β2*(Rm2-Rf) + ...

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Theory
methods_all.description: Estimates expected returns based on multiple risk factors and their betas.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Σ(Beta_i * Risk Premium_i)

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return using market beta and risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * Market Risk Premium

methods_all.name: Power Law Distribution in Financial Market Fluctuations
methods_all.description: Analyzing stock price fluctuations over time to identify patterns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: The exponent for stock returns is roughly 3.

methods_all.name: Option Pricing Theory
methods_all.description: Determines the value of options based on underlying asset values and strike prices.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Present Value of Expected Cash Flows + Contingent Events Adjustment

methods_all.name: Call Option Payoff
methods_all.description: Net payoff is gross payoff minus call price.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net Payoff = Gross Payoff - Call Price

methods_all.name: Put Option Payoff
methods_all.description: Net profit from put option is gross profit minus initial cost of the put.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net Profit = (Strike Price - Market Value) - Initial Cost

methods_all.name: Option Pricing
methods_all.description: Determines how changes in underlying asset value and variance affect call and put options.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calls increase in value with asset price, puts decrease; higher variance increases option value for both calls and puts; dividends decrease call value and increase put value.

methods_all.name: Option Value and Strike Price
methods_all.description: In calls, higher strike price decreases value; in puts, higher strike price increases value.

methods_all.name: Time to Expiration Impact
methods_all.description: Both call and put values increase with longer time to expiration due to more potential for underlying asset movement.

methods_all.name: Option Valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates the value of options based on various factors.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of exercise price, interest rates, time to expiration affect call and put values differently.

methods_all.name: Binomial Model
methods_all.description: A model that values options based on the probability of two possible stock price movements.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: S is the current stock price; Su with probability p and Sd with probability (1-p) in any time period.

methods_all.name: Binomial Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Uses risk-free borrowing/lending and underlying asset to replicate option cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C = ΔSu + (1+r)^-t(B - ΔSd)

methods_all.name: Binomial Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: A method to value options by constructing a replicating portfolio of stocks and bonds.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value the call option iteratively from the end nodes, considering stock price movements and interest rates.

methods_all.name: Binomial Model
methods_all.description: A model that provides insight into the determinants of option value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of an option is determined by current price, not expected price. If deviation from replicating portfolio exists, arbitrage opportunity arises.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model
methods_all.description: Model derived when binomial model's time periods approach zero with continuous price process.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimates the value of any option using a small number of inputs.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model
methods_all.description: Estimates the value of options using specific inputs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C = SN(d1) - Ke^(-rt)N(d2)

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model
methods_all.description: A model for pricing options contracts.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: N(d1), N(d2), continuous time present value, annualized variance

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model
methods_all.description: Calculates theoretical price of European call options.

methods_all.name: Present Value Adjustment for Short-term Options
methods_all.description: Estimate and subtract the present value of expected dividends from the asset's current value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: S = Current Asset Value - Present Value of Expected Dividends

methods_all.name: Dividend Yield Adjustment for Long-term Options
methods_all.description: Adjust the Black-Scholes model using a constant dividend yield if it remains unchanged over the option life.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: y = Dividends/Current value of the asset

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model with Dividend Adjustments
methods_all.description: Modifies the original Black-Scholes formula to account for dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjusts asset price and interest rate in the model based on dividend yield.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes model
methods_all.description: Calculates the value of a short-term option.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses dividend yield and risk-free rate as inputs.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes Model Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusting Black-Scholes model for early exercise.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value option at each potential exercise date, considering dividends and interest rates.

methods_all.name: Pseudo-American Valuation
methods_all.description: Uses Black-Scholes model to value options on ex-dividend days and chooses maximum call values.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Step 1: Define dividend payments. Step 2: Value call option using dividend-adjusted stock price for each ex-dividend day. Step 3: Choose the highest call value.

methods_all.name: Modified Binomial Model
methods_all.description: Estimates up and down movements of asset prices based on variance to consider early exercise.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses variance in ln(stock prices) to estimate movements, adjusting for riskless rate and dividends.

methods_all.name: Binomial Model
methods_all.description: A method to value options by considering cash flows at each time period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Convert variance in ln(stock prices) into inputs for the binomial model using u and d movements per unit time, specify dividend payment periods, value call option at each node allowing early exercise if remaining time premium is less than expected drop due to dividends.

methods_all.name: Binomial Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Estimate parameters for a binomial model from inputs to a Black-Scholes model.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use standard binomial approach to value call options at time 0.

methods_all.name: Warrant Valuation Iteration
methods_all.description: Iterate warrant value estimation until convergence.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Start with an assumed warrant value, estimate dilution-adjusted S, then reestimate warrant’s value using this new S.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates the theoretical price of European put and call options.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C = SN(d1) - Xe^{-rT}N(d2)

methods_all.name: Put-Call Parity
methods_all.description: Relationship between put and call options with same strike price and expiration date.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = C - S + Ke^(-rt)

methods_all.name: Put-Call Parity
methods_all.description: A relationship between the price of a European call option and put option with the same strike price and expiration date.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C + K e^{-rt} = P + S

methods_all.name: Jump Diffusion Model
methods_all.description: An extension to the Black-Scholes model that incorporates jumps in stock prices, allowing for more realistic modeling of sudden price changes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of an option depends on five variables from Black-Scholes and parameters of jump process (λ, k)

methods_all.name: Capped Call Option Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing a capped call option involves calculating the difference between the value of an unrestricted call and one with a cap.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Capped Call = Value of Unrestricted Call - Value of Call with Cap

methods_all.name: Geske's analytical formulation
methods_all.description: Valuing compound options using a bivariate normal distribution.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Replace the standard normal distribution with a bivariate normal distribution in option valuation.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes model
methods_all.description: Used to estimate the value of options and replicating call positions.

methods_all.name: Black-Scholes model
methods_all.description: Used for valuing options based on underlying asset price, volatility, time to expiration, risk-free rate, and dividend yield.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: C = SN(d1) - Ke^(-rT)N(d2)

methods_all.name: Efficiency Testing
methods_all.description: Testing investment schemes to prove or disprove market efficiency.

methods_all.name: Random Diversification
methods_all.description: Diversifying across stocks or indexing to the market.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Minimize information and execution costs.

methods_all.name: Minimal Trading Strategy
methods_all.description: Creating a portfolio and not trading unless cash was needed.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Superior to frequent trading strategies in efficient markets.

methods_all.name: Proposition 1
methods_all.description: The probability of finding inefficiencies in an asset market decreases as the ease of trading on the asset increases.

methods_all.name: Proposition 2
methods_all.description: The probability of finding an inefficiency in an asset market increases as the transactions and information cost of exploiting the inefficiency increases.

methods_all.name: Proposition 3
methods_all.description: The speed with which an inefficiency is resolved will be directly related to how easily the scheme to exploit the inefficiency can be replicated by other investors.

methods_all.name: Event Study
methods_all.description: Examines market reactions to specific information events.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Identify event, collect returns around the event date, determine return interval and window size based on precision of event date.

methods_all.name: Event Study Methodology
methods_all.description: Calculates abnormal returns around an event date.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ERjt = Rjt - E(Rjt), t-statistic = average excess return / standard error

methods_all.name: t statistic estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate whether event affects stock returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Divide average excess return by standard error to get t statistic.

methods_all.name: Portfolio Study
methods_all.description: A method to test investment strategies by creating portfolios of firms with specific characteristics and examining their returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula:
1. Define the variable for classification based on the investment strategy.
2. Collect data at the start of the testing period and classify firms into portfolios.
3. Calculate portfolio returns over the testing period, either equally weighted or value-weighted.
4. Estimate beta or betas for each portfolio.

methods_all.name: Portfolio Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate portfolio beta by averaging individual stock betas or regressing portfolio returns against market returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = Covariance(portfolio returns, market returns) / Variance(market returns)

methods_all.name: Avoiding Anecdotal Evidence
methods_all.description: Do not use anecdotal evidence to support or reject an investment strategy due to its inherent bias and noise in stock prices.

methods_all.name: Out-of-Sample Testing
methods_all.description: Always test an investment strategy on a different time period or universe than the one from which it was derived to avoid overfitting.

methods_all.name: Controlling for Market Performance
methods_all.description: Adjusting investment scheme results to account for overall market performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare returns against a benchmark index.

methods_all.name: Risk Adjustment
methods_all.description: Evaluating investment schemes based on risk-adjusted returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate Sharpe Ratio or Information Ratio.

methods_all.name: Survival bias
methods_all.description: Bias in research due to elimination of failed firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None

methods_all.name: Transaction cost consideration
methods_all.description: Adjusting returns for transaction costs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None

methods_all.name: Transaction Cost Analysis
methods_all.description: Evaluating the impact of transaction costs on investment strategy returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Excess Returns - Transaction Costs >= Required Return

methods_all.name: Serial Correlation
methods_all.description: Measures correlation between price changes in consecutive time periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: A positive serial correlation suggests momentum, while a negative one indicates reversals. Strategies can be devised based on these correlations to potentially earn excess returns.

methods_all.name: Filter Rule
methods_all.description: Buys an investment if the price rises X percent from a previous low and holds until the price drops X percent from a previous high.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: X percent change triggers trade, where X varies by rule.

methods_all.name: 0.5 percent rule
methods_all.description: A stock is bought when it rises 0.5% from a previous low and sold when it falls 0.5% from a prior high.

methods_all.name: Price Momentum
methods_all.description: Stocks that have gone up in the last few weeks or months tend to continue to outperform, and stocks that have gone down tend to underperform.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Jegadeesh and Titman (1993, 2001) present evidence of price momentum over time periods of up to eight months.

methods_all.name: Price Reversal Strategy
methods_all.description: Construct portfolios based on past performance to predict future returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Create a 'winner' portfolio of stocks that have performed well in the prior year and a 'loser' portfolio of stocks that have underperformed, then examine their relative performance over the following 60 months.

methods_all.name: loser portfolio analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing portfolios of underperforming stocks.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Consider the impact of low-priced stocks and transaction costs on returns.

methods_all.name: Speculative Bubble Formation
methods_all.description: Formation of speculative bubbles due to mispricing and uncertainty.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Not applicable

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates asset value based on expected future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t)

methods_all.name: Earnings Report Analysis
methods_all.description: Measuring market reaction to earnings surprises.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Market reacts instantaneously to surprising information in earnings reports, with positive surprises leading to price increases and negative surprises to declines.

methods_all.name: Earnings Report Timing Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing the day of the week for earnings reports to predict market reactions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Negative information is more likely in Friday announcements compared to other days.

methods_all.name: Market Reaction Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing abnormal returns to investment announcements.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate cumulative abnormal returns (CAR) around announcement dates.

methods_all.name: Size Effect
methods_all.description: Investing in smaller firms can yield higher returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Risk-adjusted returns for small cap stocks are generally higher than larger firms.

methods_all.name: Small Firm Premium
methods_all.description: Additional risk and return associated with small stocks.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimate the premium by comparing returns of small vs large firms over a period.

methods_all.name: Price-Earnings Ratio Screening
methods_all.description: Identifying undervalued stocks based on low PE ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Screen for stocks in the lowest PE ratio class to find potential excess return opportunities.

methods_all.name: Low PE Ratio Strategy
methods_all.description: Investing in stocks with low price-earnings ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Select stocks from the bottom quintile of PE ratios.

methods_all.name: Price–Book Value Ratio Strategy
methods_all.description: Investing in stocks with low price-to-book value ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Choose firms with high book-value to market-value ratios.

methods_all.name: Price-Book Value Ratio Analysis
methods_all.description: Evaluating stocks based on their price-to-book value ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate the return differential of low price–book value portfolios against market indices.

methods_all.name: January Effect Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing stock returns in January by size and risk class.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Returns in January for small firms are significantly higher than other months, with half of the premium earned in the first two weeks.

methods_all.name: January Effect
methods_all.description: Institutional buying/selling patterns around year-end affect stock prices.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Prices drop before the turn of the year and rise after due to institutional behavior.

methods_all.name: Weekend Effect
methods_all.description: Returns on Mondays differ significantly from other days of the week over long periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Average daily returns show a pattern with higher returns on Mondays compared to other weekdays.

methods_all.name: Weekend Effect Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing returns on Mondays and over weekends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate average daily returns for each day of the week, focusing on Monday and weekend periods.

methods_all.name: Insider Trading Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing insider trading patterns to predict stock performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare stocks where insiders have bought more than sold ('buy group') versus those where sells exceed buys ('sell group').

methods_all.name: Analyst Recommendations Impact
methods_all.description: Analyzing the impact of buy and sell recommendations on stock prices.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Buy recommendations lead to a 3% price increase, while sell recommendations cause a 4% drop initially. Sell recommendations also result in an additional 5% decline over six months.

methods_all.name: Jensen's Study
methods_all.description: Analyzes mutual fund performance relative to market.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Excess returns on mutual funds were negative between 1955 and 1964.

methods_all.name: quartile classification
methods_all.description: Classifies money managers into quartiles based on performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: N/A

methods_all.name: Fama-French analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing mutual fund performance net and gross of costs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net returns show little evidence of outperformance; gross returns suggest some skill, with superior managers generating about 1.25% more than average.

methods_all.name: Transaction Cost Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing costs associated with executing trades.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Total cost = (Bid-ask spread + Commission) * Number of transactions

methods_all.name: Event Study Methodology
methods_all.description: Analyzing stock price reactions to specific events.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Step 1: Identify significant mergers. Step 2: Determine key event dates. Step 3: Analyze returns around these dates.

methods_all.name: Tax Effect Hypothesis
methods_all.description: Explanation of the January effect related to tax year-end activities.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None provided, but involves understanding how tax considerations influence investor behavior at year-end.

methods_all.name: Indifference Point Calculation
methods_all.description: Determine the capital gains tax rate that makes an investor indifferent between two portfolios given a dividend yield and expected price appreciation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (1 - Dividend Tax Rate) * Expected Dividend Yield + (1 - Capital Gains Tax Rate) * Expected Price Appreciation = Constant Return

methods_all.name: Risk-Adjusted Performance
methods_all.description: Evaluates fund performance after adjusting for risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Fund Return - Risk-Free Rate) / Standard Deviation of Fund Returns

methods_all.name: Break-even Analysis
methods_all.description: Calculates the time required to recover initial investment losses due to front-end loads.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Initial Investment Loss / Annual Excess Return

methods_all.name: F test
methods_all.description: Tests for equality of means across groups.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Can be conducted assuming either that the groups have the same variance or that they have different variances.

methods_all.name: Rank sum test
methods_all.description: Ranks returns across the entire sample and then sums the ranks within each group to check whether the rankings are random or systematic.

methods_all.name: CAPM
methods_all.description: Model that calculates the expected return on an investment based on its beta and market returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_all.name: Multifactor Models
methods_all.description: Models that consider multiple sources of risk to estimate premiums for specific factors.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Σ(Beta_i * Factor Premium_i)

methods_all.name: Duration Matching Strategy
methods_all.description: A method to match the duration of default-free security used as risk-free asset with the duration of cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use a government bond rate that matches the time horizon of the investment or valuation.

methods_all.name: Real versus Nominal Risk-Free Rates
methods_all.description: Valuation under high and unstable inflation conditions using real growth rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cash flows are estimated using real growth rates, discount rates must be real. Real risk-free rate = nominal interest rate - expected inflation.

methods_all.name: Local Currency Government Bond Method
methods_all.description: Uses long-term bonds denominated in local currency to estimate risk-free rate, adjusting for default risk using sovereign ratings or CDS.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Risk-Free Rate = Local Bond Yield - Default Spread (from rating or CDS)

methods_all.name: Interest Rate Parity
methods_all.description: Calculates forward rates based on spot rates and interest rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Forward rate = Spot rate * (1 + foreign risk-free rate) / (1 + domestic risk-free rate)

methods_all.name: Risk-Free Rate Conversion
methods_all.description: Converts risk-free rates between currencies using expected inflation differences.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Emerging market risk-free rate = Mature market risk-free rate + Expected inflation difference

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: A model that measures market risk using a single factor, beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * Equity Risk Premium

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Theory (APT)
methods_all.description: A model that measures market risk using multiple unspecified factors.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Sum(Beta_j * Risk_Premium_j)

methods_all.name: Multifactor Model
methods_all.description: A model that uses specified macroeconomic factors to measure market risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Sum(Beta_j * Risk_Premium_j)

methods_all.name: Historical Risk Premium Approach
methods_all.description: Estimate the risk premium by comparing historical returns of stocks to default-free securities over long periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Annual difference between stock returns and government security returns

methods_all.name: Standard Error Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the standard error of risk premium estimates based on different time periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Standard error increases with shorter estimation periods due to higher noise in stock prices.

methods_all.name: Risk-Free Rate Selection
methods_all.description: Determining appropriate risk-free rate for calculating equity risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If Treasury bill rate is used, calculate stock premium over T-bills. If Treasury bond rate is preferred, compute premium relative to long-term government bonds.

methods_all.name: Arithmetic and Geometric Averages
methods_all.description: Measuring average returns on stocks, Treasury bonds, and bills.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Arithmetic average: simple mean of annual returns; Geometric average: compounded return over time.

methods_all.name: Geometric Average Risk Premium
methods_all.description: Calculates the geometric average risk premium for stocks over Treasury bonds.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: 4.31 percent, the geometric average risk premium from 1928 to 2010

methods_all.name: Modified Historical Risk Premium
methods_all.description: Estimating risk premiums for mature and emerging markets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Risk premium = Base premium + Country premium

methods_all.name: Country Bond Default Spreads
methods_all.description: Estimating risk premium based on bond default spreads.

methods_all.name: Equity Market Volatility
methods_all.description: Assessing country risk through equity market volatility.

methods_all.name: Default Risk Spreads
methods_all.description: Uses sovereign ratings and CDS spreads to estimate default risk premiums.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Average the spreads within each ratings class to arrive at the average spread for each sovereign rating.

methods_all.name: Cost of Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the cost of equity for a company by adding default spreads to the U.S. risk premium and adjusting with beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (U.S. Treasury bond rate + (default spread + U.S. risk premium) * beta)

methods_all.name: Relative Standard Deviation Method
methods_all.description: A method to calculate total risk premium by scaling standard deviation of one market against another.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Total Risk Premium = (Standard Deviation of Market X / Standard Deviation of Base Market) * Base Market's Risk Premium

methods_all.name: Country Equity Risk Premium Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimates equity risk premium based on country bond default spread and relative volatility of equity market.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Equity Volatility - Bond Volatility) + Country Default Spread

methods_all.name: Country Risk Premium Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate country risk premium based on bond spreads.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Risk premium converges to the default spread over time.

methods_all.name: Equal Exposure Assumption
methods_all.description: Assume all companies in a country are equally exposed to country risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of equity = Risk-free rate + Equity risk premium + Country risk premium

methods_all.name: Cost of Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates cost of equity for Petrobras considering country risk exposure.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Equity = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate) + λ * Country Risk Premium

methods_all.name: Country Risk Premium Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimates the country risk premium and λ to adjust cost of equity for companies with international exposure.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Equity = Expected Return Model + (λ * Country Risk Premium)

methods_all.name: Implied Equity Premiums
methods_all.description: Estimating equity risk premium without historical data.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: r = (D1/P0) + g, where r is the required return on equity, D1 is expected dividend next period, P0 is current market level, and g is long-term growth rate.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (CFt / (1 + r)^t) for t=1 to n, plus terminal value discounted back

methods_all.name: Regression Analysis
methods_all.description: Relating implied equity risk premiums to Treasury bond rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Implied Equity Premium = β0 + β1 * (Treasury Bond Rate) + ε, where R-squared is approximately 15% and t statistics indicate the significance of the independent variable.

methods_all.name: Implied Equity Premium Calculation
methods_all.description: Substituting the current Treasury bond rate into the equation to yield an updated estimate of the implied equity premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use historical data and market views to adjust the calculation.

methods_all.name: Estimating Default Spreads
methods_all.description: Identify a sample of bonds within each ratings class and estimate their interest rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use liquid bonds with few special features to reduce misrating/mispricing issues.

methods_all.name: Yield Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate interest rate on bonds using yield or yield to maturity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Yield = Coupon Rate / Market Price; Yield to Maturity solves for the rate that equates present value of cash flows to market price.

methods_all.name: Weighted Average Interest Rates
methods_all.description: Compute weighted average interest rates based on trading volume.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Weighted Average Interest Rate = Σ (Interest Rate * Trading Volume) / Total Trading Volume

methods_all.name: Default spread analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing how default spreads vary with bond maturity and economic conditions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Empirically, the default spread for corporate bonds of a given ratings class seems to increase with the maturity of the bond.

methods_all.name: Risk-Free Rate Determination
methods_all.description: Identifies criteria for selecting a risk-free rate in valuation models.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use default-free (government) zero coupon rates matched to the duration of cash flows being discounted.

methods_all.name: Implied Equity Premium Estimation
methods_all.description: Describes an alternative method to estimate equity premiums using current market perceptions and valuation models.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimate expected growth and cash flows on equity investments from a valuation model.

methods_all.name: Riskless Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating a risk-free rate for valuation purposes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use current government bond rates adjusted for credit risk and currency factors.

methods_all.name: Country Risk Premium Calculation
methods_all.description: Determining the additional return required to compensate for country-specific risks.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate using default spreads or equity market volatility relative to a benchmark like S&P 500.

methods_all.name: Implied Equity Risk Premium Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate the implied equity risk premium based on expected growth rates, dividends, and riskless rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ERP = Expected Growth Rate - Riskless Rate

methods_all.name: Compounded Return Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates compounded return using initial and final investment values.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Value_N / Value_0)^(1/N) - 1

methods_all.name: Regression equation
methods_all.description: Substituting Treasury bond rate into regression equation.

methods_all.name: Regression Estimates of Betas
methods_all.description: Estimating betas by regressing stock returns against market returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: b = Covariance(Rj, Rm)/σ²m

methods_all.name: Jensen's alpha
methods_all.description: Measures whether the investment earned a return greater than or less than its required return.

methods_all.name: Regression analysis
methods_all.description: Estimates beta and other risk parameters for a firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Slope of regression = Beta; Intercept of regression = Jensen's alpha

methods_all.name: CAPM
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return based on market risk premium and beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_all.name: Jensen's Alpha
methods_all.description: Measures the performance of an investment by comparing its actual returns to those predicted by CAPM.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Alpha = Actual Return - Expected Return

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation Method
methods_all.description: Uses price appreciation and ignores dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = Covariance(Stock Return, Market Return) / Variance(Market Return)

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation Methodology
methods_all.description: Describes the process of estimating beta using different return intervals and market indexes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use weekly or monthly returns to reduce nontrading bias. Estimate betas relative to appropriate market indexes for domestic or international investors.

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Method to estimate market risk of a company using regression analysis.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Regression equation: Beta = Covariance(R_stock, R_market) / Variance(R_market)

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Calculates stock's sensitivity to market movements.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = Covariance(Stock, Market) / Variance(Market)

methods_all.name: Historical Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating beta for assets with market price history.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Regression analysis using historical returns of the asset and a benchmark index.

methods_all.name: Proxy Method
methods_all.description: Using publicly traded firms as proxies to estimate betas for private companies or recently public firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Selecting comparable firms based on industry, size, and risk profile.

methods_all.name: Regression Analysis
methods_all.description: Estimate betas for companies by running a regression of stock returns against market returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Stock Return = Beta * Market Return + Error Term

methods_all.name: Regression Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Run a regression of stock returns against market returns to estimate risk parameters.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Stock return = alpha + beta * Market return + error

methods_all.name: Determinants of Betas
methods_all.description: Identifies three variables that determine a firm's beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (1) Type of business, (2) Degree of operating leverage, and (3) Financial leverage.

methods_all.name: Degree of Financial Leverage
methods_all.description: Calculates the effect of financial leverage on equity beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: βL = βu + (D/E) * (1 - t) * (βu - βd)

methods_all.name: Estimating Unlevered Beta
methods_all.description: Calculates unlevered beta using historical data and tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Beta = Levered Beta / (1 + (1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity)

methods_all.name: Bottom-Up Betas
methods_all.description: Estimating firm beta by breaking down into business risk and financial leverage components.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta of two assets = weighted average of individual asset betas based on market value

methods_all.name: Bottom-Up Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: A method to estimate a firm's beta by averaging betas from similar publicly traded firms and adjusting for leverage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Step-by-step process including regression beta calculation, unlevering average beta, estimating unlevered beta for the firm being analyzed, and leveraging the final beta.

methods_all.name: Standard Error Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates standard error of average beta using sample size.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: standard error = average standard error / sqrt(n)

methods_all.name: Bottom-up betas
methods_all.description: Adjusts beta estimates based on current and future debt-to-equity ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = (1 + (Debt/Equity)) * Beta unlevered

methods_all.name: Adjusting Betas for Cash Holdings
methods_all.description: When estimating betas, adjust for the proportion of a firm's assets that are in cash by using a beta of zero for cash.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered beta for business = (Sector-average unlevered beta) / ((1 - Proportion of cash))

methods_all.name: Bottom-Up Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating beta for a company by considering betas of publicly traded companies in the same sector.
methods_all.rules_or_formula:
Unlevered beta = Levered beta / (1 + (1 - tax rate) * debt/equity ratio)
Levered beta = Unlevered beta * (1 + (1 - tax rate) * debt/equity ratio)

methods_all.name: Bottom-Up Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate beta by breaking down the company's business into different areas and calculating their betas separately.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = Covariance(Rm, Rj) / Variance(Rm)

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating beta for a company by breaking it into business segments and finding comparable firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Beta = Levered Beta / (1 + ((1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity))

methods_all.name: Bottom-up beta estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating a firm's beta after major restructuring by considering changes in business mix and leverage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered beta of combined firm = weighted average of unlevered betas of constituent firms, weights based on market values.

methods_all.name: Unlevered Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate the unlevered beta for a combined firm as the weighted average of two firms' unlevered betas.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Beta (Combined Firm) = Weighted Average of Unlevered Betas

methods_all.name: Accounting Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate market risk parameters from accounting earnings changes relative to market earnings changes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Accounting Beta = Change in Accounting Earnings / Change in Market Earnings

methods_all.name: Regression Analysis
methods_all.description: Regressing changes in profits of the defense division against changes in profits for the S&P 500.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Based on this regression, the beta for the defense division is 0.65.

methods_all.name: regression analysis
methods_all.description: Estimate beta for divisions or firms using regression.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = slope from regression of asset returns against market returns.

methods_all.name: accounting beta estimation
methods_all.description: Use accounting data to estimate betas.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Spreadsheet tool: accbeta.xls

methods_all.name: bottom-up approach
methods_all.description: Calculate betas by considering business and financial mix changes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Average betas across large numbers of firms, less noisy than individual firm betas.

methods_all.name: Revenue breakdown
methods_all.description: Estimate lambda using the proportion of a firm's revenues generated in a country relative to the average firm in that country.

methods_all.name: lambda estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate lambda by comparing firm's revenue proportion in a country to the average firm's revenue proportion in that country.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (firm's revenue from country / average firm's revenue from country)

methods_all.name: regression method
methods_all.description: Run regression of stock returns for each firm against the returns on the country bond to estimate lambda.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: slope of regression line representing sensitivity of company's stock price to changes in country risk

methods_all.name: lambda estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate lambdas for companies with significant foreign market exposure.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Lambda = (Revenues from risky markets / Total revenues) * Risk premium of risky markets

methods_all.name: CAPM
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return using riskless rate, beta, and risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Riskless Rate + Beta * (Risk Premium)

methods_all.name: Arbitrage Pricing Theory & Multifactor Model
methods_all.description: Incorporates multiple factors to estimate expected returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Riskless Rate + Σ(β_j * risk premium_j)

methods_all.name: Cost of Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimating cost of equity for Embraer in USD and converting it into nominal Brazilian real.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Treasury bond rate + country risk premium + market risk premium - tax adjusted beta

methods_all.name: Adjusting Betas for Operating Leverage
methods_all.description: Adjust the betas of small firms to account for higher operating leverage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Higher beta = Beta + (Operating Leverage Factor * Beta) - Original Beta

methods_all.name: Calculating After-Tax Cost of Debt
methods_all.description: Determine the after-tax cost of debt by considering riskless rate, default spread, and tax advantage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: After-Tax Cost of Debt = Pretax Cost of Debt * (1 - Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Cost of Debt Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate costs of debt using ratings and default spreads.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Debt = Treasury bond rate + Default Spread (e.g., A-rated firm's cost of debt is approximately 1.00% higher than the Treasury bond rate)

methods_all.name: Estimate a synthetic rating
methods_all.description: Assign a rating to a firm based on its financial ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use interest coverage ratios and S&P ratings class ranges to determine the synthetic rating.

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio Rating
methods_all.description: Determines bond ratings based on interest coverage ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Rating is assigned based on the Interest Coverage Ratio with specific spreads for each rating level.

methods_all.name: Synthetic Ratings Approach
methods_all.description: Develops a score based on multiple financial ratios to estimate synthetic ratings and default spread.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Incorporates Altman Z score or other financial ratios weighted to generate a score, which is then related to bond ratings.

methods_all.name: Tax Rate Estimation for After-Tax Cost of Debt
methods_all.description: Estimates the after-tax cost of debt by multiplying pretax cost with (1 - tax rate), using marginal tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (After-tax cost of debt) = Pretax cost * (1 - Marginal Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Estimating Cost of Debt
methods_all.description: Calculate pretax cost by adding default spread to riskless rate, then apply tax rate for after-tax cost.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Pretax cost = Riskless rate + Default spread; After-tax cost = Pretax cost * (1 - Tax rate)

methods_all.name: Cost of Debt Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates after-tax cost of debt for Embraer using Treasury bond rate and default spread.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: After-Tax Cost of Debt = Pretax Cost of Debt * (1 - Marginal Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Cost of Preferred Stock Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimating the cost of preferred stock using a perpetual dividend model.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Preferred Stock = Dividend / Price

methods_all.name: Convertible Bond Decomposition
methods_all.description: Breaks down convertible bonds into debt and equity components for cost calculation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Straight bond component is treated as debt, conversion option as equity.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing Operating Leases
methods_all.description: Convert operating lease expenses into a debt equivalent.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discount future lease payments using the firm's pretax cost of borrowing as the discount rate.

methods_all.name: Present Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of future lease expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: PV = FV / (1 + r)^n

methods_all.name: Market Value Debt Ratio Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates market value debt ratio using firm's cost of equity and after-tax cost of debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Capital = (E/V) * Re + (D/V) * Rd * (1-Tc), where E is the market value of equity, V is total market value of capital, Re is cost of equity, D is market value of debt, Rd is after-tax cost of debt, Tc is corporate tax rate.

methods_all.name: Market Value Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating market value of debt using book value, interest expense, maturity, and cost of debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Market Value = Present Value of Interest Payments + Present Value of Principal Repayment

methods_all.name: Net Debt Ratios
methods_all.description: Calculating net debt by subtracting cash from total debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net Debt = Total Debt - Cash

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates weighted average cost of capital using market values of equity, debt, and preferred stock.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: WACC = (E/V) * k_e + (D/V) * k_d * (1 - T_c) + (PS/V) * k_ps

methods_all.name: Levered Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Incorporating cash into beta computation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = (Unlevered Beta * (1 + (Debt/Equity) * (1 - Tax Rate)))

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the weighted average cost of capital based on market values of debt and equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: WACC = (E/V) * Re + (D/V) * Rd * (1 - Tc)

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: Calculates expected return based on market risk premium
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Estimates stock price based on future dividends
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Stock Price = Dividend / (Discount Rate - Growth Rate)

methods_all.name: Degree of Operating Leverage
methods_all.description: Measures how sensitive operating income is to changes in sales.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Operating Income / Earnings Before Interest and Taxes) * (Sales / Operating Income)

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation via Regression Analysis
methods_all.description: Estimates the beta of a stock by regressing its returns against market index returns over a specified period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: beta = covariance(stock_returns, market_returns) / variance(market_returns)

methods_all.name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
methods_all.description: Calculating required return based on market risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_all.name: Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Determining the beta of a stock or firm using regression analysis and R-squared values.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = (Covariance(Stock Returns, Market Returns)) / Variance(Market Returns)

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Calculating unlevered beta and adjusting for leverage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Beta = Levered Beta / (1 + ((1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity))

methods_all.name: Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate beta for a company based on its debt-to-equity ratio and comparable firms' betas.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = Unlevered Beta * (1 + (1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity)

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate the cost of capital for a firm using its beta, market value of debt, and tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Capital = Weight of Equity * Cost of Equity + Weight of Debt * Cost of Debt * (1 - Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: CAPM Regression Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing regression intercept to assess risk-adjusted performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Intercept should be zero if actual returns equal expected CAPM returns, positive if better than expected, negative if worse.

methods_all.name: Hamada formula modification
methods_all.description: Adjusts beta for tax effects and market risk of debt.

methods_all.name: Adjusting Accounting Earnings
methods_all.description: Method to adjust accounting earnings for valuation purposes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust operating lease expenses as financial expenses and R&D expenses as capital expenses.

methods_all.name: Updating Earnings
methods_all.description: Use trailing 12-month revenues and earnings for more recent estimates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Aggregate quarterly reports over the most recent four quarters to estimate updated financial metrics.

methods_all.name: Updating Valuation Inputs
