methods_all.description: Use most recent available data for valuation, especially for rapidly growing firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If revenues have doubled, assume options outstanding may also have doubled.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing R&D Expenses
methods_all.description: A method for adjusting financial statements to correctly classify capital expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimate the amortizable life of research and development (R&D) assets based on the time it takes to convert R&D into commercial products. Collect historical R&D expense data over this period, assuming uniform amortization to estimate residual value.

methods_all.name: Uniform Amortization Method
methods_all.description: Estimate residual value of research asset.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cumulate fractions of R&D expenses from previous years to current year's entire expense.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing R&D Expenses
methods_all.description: Converts R&D expenses from operating to capital expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Determine amortizable life for R&D, collect historical data based on this period.

methods_all.name: Amortization of R&D Expenses
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of research assets and amortize R&D expenses over a 10-year period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: R&D expense is amortized linearly at 10% per year for 10 years. Current year's expenditure is not amortized until the next period.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing SG&A Expenses
methods_all.description: A method to treat certain operating expenses as capital expenses for valuation purposes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Determine the period over which benefits accrue, estimate asset value created by these expenses, adjust operating income and net income.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing Recruitment and Training Expenses
methods_all.description: Adjusting financial statements by capitalizing recruitment and training costs over a period of time.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Amortize the cost of recruiting new consultants and training expenses over four years.

methods_all.name: Converting Operating Leases into Debt
methods_all.description: Recategorizing operating lease commitments as debt to adjust for financial reporting accuracy.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discount future operating lease commitments back at the firm's pretax cost of debt, then add this present value to conventional debt.

methods_all.name: Adjusting Operating Income for Operating Leases
methods_all.description: Add back operating lease expenses and subtract out depreciation on leased assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Operating Income = Operating Income + Operating Lease Expense - Depreciation on Leased Asset

methods_all.name: Adjustment for Imputed Interest Expense
methods_all.description: Estimate the adjustment by adding imputed interest expense using pretax cost of debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Imputed Interest Expense = Pretax Cost of Debt * Adjusted Operating Lease Expenses

methods_all.name: Whispered Earnings Estimation
methods_all.description: Firms aim to beat earnings estimates that are slightly higher than official analyst predictions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beat whispered estimates = Actual EPS > Whispered EPS

methods_all.name: Booking revenues early
methods_all.description: Shipping products at the end of a weak quarter to record revenues.

methods_all.name: Swapping revenues
methods_all.description: Two firms enter into a transaction swapping revenues to boost each other's reported earnings.

methods_all.name: Capitalizing operating expenses
methods_all.description: Classifying expenses as capital rather than operating to defer costs and improve earnings.

methods_all.name: Write-offs
methods_all.description: Taking major restructuring charges to separate expenses from operations and boost future earnings.

methods_all.name: Estimating Operating and Net Income
methods_all.description: Exclude one-time or extraordinary items from projections.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Operating income should reflect continuing operations without one-time expenses or income.

methods_all.name: Normalization Method
methods_all.description: Averaging expenses across time to reduce income by this amount.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Average annual expense = Sum of all yearly expenses / Number of years. Adjust current year's income by subtracting average annual expense.

methods_all.name: Earnings Prior to Write-off
methods_all.description: Adjust earnings before considering noncash charges like in-process R&D write-offs.

methods_all.name: Net Capital Expenditures Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate net capital expenditures by subtracting divestiture cash flows, net of taxes, from gross capital expenditures.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net CapEx = Gross CapEx - (Divestitures - Taxes on Divestitures)

methods_all.name: Valuation Method for Passive Holdings
methods_all.description: Value operating assets separately and add value of passive holding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Holding Inc. = Value of Operating Assets + (Ownership Percentage * Value of Subsidiary Inc.)

methods_all.name: Consolidation Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing consolidated firms by adjusting for minority interests or stripping subsidiary income.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Consolidated Firm = Value of Operating Income - Minority Interests

methods_all.name: Adjustment Method
methods_all.description: Adjust earnings by adding back one-time charges and averaging recurring but volatile expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Net Income = Reported Net Income + Inventory Charge + Restructuring Charges + After-Tax Portion of Other Expenses - Average Annual Expense Over Three Years

methods_all.name: Earnings Correction Method
methods_all.description: Correcting earnings for misclassified operating leases and R&D expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust reported earnings by reclassifying operating leases as financing expenses and R&D expenses as capital expenses.

methods_all.name: Debt Value of Operating Leases
methods_all.description: Estimate the debt value of operating leases and calculate the book value debt-to-equity ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of lease payments at current cost of borrowing.

methods_all.name: Adjusted Operating Income
methods_all.description: Calculate adjusted operating income by capitalizing operating leases.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Operating income before interest and taxes + present value of lease commitments - operating lease expense.

methods_all.name: Tax Benefit Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating the tax benefit from R&D expenses being tax deductible.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (R&D – Amortization)(1 – Tax rate) + differential tax benefit

methods_all.name: Revenue Recognition
methods_all.description: How firms recognize revenue from software sales and licensing agreements.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Revenue should be recognized when it is earned, not necessarily when cash is received.

methods_all.name: After-tax Operating Income Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating after-tax operating income from earnings before interest and taxes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Multiply EBIT by an estimated tax rate.

methods_all.name: Tax Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating effective tax rates for firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Effective Tax Rate = Total Taxes Paid / Total Income

methods_all.name: weighted average of marginal tax rates
methods_all.description: Calculates a weighted average based on income from each country.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Weights change over time if income growth rates differ.

methods_all.name: marginal tax rate of incorporation country
methods_all.description: Uses the marginal tax rate where the company is incorporated, assuming repatriation.

methods_all.name: separate income streams
methods_all.description: Applies different marginal tax rates to each income stream from separate countries.

methods_all.name: Effective Tax Rate Forever
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of Convoy assuming a constant tax rate of 20%.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value based on deferred taxes never being paid.

methods_all.name: Marginal Tax Rate Forever
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of Convoy assuming a marginal tax rate of 40%. Adjust for cumulated deferred taxes if applicable.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of deferred tax payments = $50 million(PV of annuity, 9%, 4 years) = $161.99 million; Firm value adjusted by present value of deferred tax payments.

methods_all.name: Blended Tax Rates
methods_all.description: Use effective tax rate for the first five years and marginal tax rate thereafter to compute terminal value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Deferred tax liability increases with use of effective tax rate; Payments spread over 10 years starting year 6, discounted back an additional five years.

methods_all.name: Tax Rate Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjust tax rates over time based on net operating losses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: In early years, firms with large NOLs will have a zero tax rate. As NOLs decrease, the tax rate climbs toward the marginal tax rate.

methods_all.name: Tax Benefits of R&D Expensing
methods_all.description: Adjust after-tax operating income by adding the tax savings on the difference between R&D expense and amortized amount.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: After-tax operating income = (pretax operating income - amortization) * (1 – Tax rate) + additional tax benefit from expensing

methods_all.name: Tax Benefit Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the difference between expensing and capitalizing R&D for tax benefit.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Differential Tax Benefit = (R&D Expense - Amortization) * Tax Rate

methods_all.name: Tax Subsidies Valuation Method
methods_all.description: Value the firm ignoring tax subsidies, then add value increment from subsidies.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Valuation = Base Valuation + Present Value of Tax Savings

methods_all.name: Normalization of Capital Expenditures
methods_all.description: Averaging capital expenditures over a number of years.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimate the average capital expenditures over the past four or five years for a manufacturing firm and use that number rather than the most recent year's expenditure.

methods_all.name: Normalization of Net Capital Expenditures
methods_all.description: A method to adjust for volatility in capital expenditures.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use the average capital expenditure over a period and depreciation from a specific year to normalize net capital expenditures.

methods_all.name: Capitalization of R&D
methods_all.description: Amortizing R&D expenses over a period to estimate the value of research assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Research Asset = Total R&D Expense * Amortizable Life

methods_all.name: Adjusting Net Capital Expenditures
methods_all.description: Incorporating R&D expenses and acquisitions into net capital expenditures.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: - Adjusted Net CapEx = Net CapEx (from financials) + R&D Expenses - Amortization of R&D + Acquisitions

methods_all.name: valuation consistency
methods_all.description: Ensure internal consistency in valuation when ignoring acquisitions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Do not consider the growth due to acquisitions if they are not included in the valuation.

methods_all.name: valuation methodology
methods_all.description: Adjusting working capital for valuation purposes by excluding cash and marketable securities from current assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Working Capital = Current Assets - Cash - Marketable Securities - Current Liabilities + Interest-Bearing Debt Due in the Current Period

methods_all.name: Estimating Expected Changes in Noncash Working Capital
methods_all.description: Use financial statements to estimate changes, but tie these to expected revenue or cost of goods sold changes over time.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Changes in noncash working capital should be tied to expected changes in revenues or costs of goods sold at the firm over time.

methods_all.name: Noncash Working Capital Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate changes in noncash working capital based on historical data and expected revenue growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use the change in noncash working capital as a percent of revenues from recent years to forecast future changes, adjusting for business trends or shifts.

methods_all.name: Working Capital Projection Method
methods_all.description: Estimates working capital based on industry average as a percent of revenues.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Noncash Working Capital = Industry Average % * Revenues

methods_all.name: Negative Working Capital Strategy
methods_all.description: Firms using supplier credit as a source of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare costs of this capital to more traditional forms of borrowing.

methods_all.name: After-tax Operating Income Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating after-tax operating income using effective tax rates in early years and marginal tax rates in future periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Effective tax rate for early years, Marginal tax rate for future periods

methods_all.name: Effective Tax Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates after-tax operating income using the effective tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: After-Tax Operating Income = Earnings Before Taxes * (1 - Effective Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Marginal Tax Rate Application
methods_all.description: Estimates after-tax operating income based on the marginal tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: After-Tax Operating Income = Earnings Before Taxes * (1 - Marginal Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Estimate Free Cash Flows
methods_all.description: Calculate free cash flows to the firm based on revenue growth and working capital changes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Free Cash Flow = Operating Income + Depreciation & Amortization - Capital Expenditures - Increase in Working Capital

methods_all.name: Historical Growth Rate
methods_all.description: Use past earnings growth rate as an input for valuation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Not recommended for high-growth firms due to estimation difficulties and unreliability.

methods_all.name: Analyst Estimates
methods_all.description: Rely on analysts' estimates of future growth rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Poor quality over longer periods, leading to inconsistent valuations.

methods_all.name: Fundamental Growth Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate growth based on reinvestment and investment quality.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Involves assessing new asset investments including acquisitions and marketing capabilities.

methods_all.name: Arithmetic versus Geometric Averages
methods_all.description: Calculating average growth rates using arithmetic or geometric means.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Geometric mean takes into account compounding over time: g = (1 + g1) * (1 + g2) * ... * (1 + gn)^(1/n) - 1

methods_all.name: OLS regression
methods_all.description: Measures earnings change per time period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EPS_t = a + b*t

methods_all.name: Log-linear OLS regression
methods_all.description: Converts coefficient into percentage change for growth projection.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ln(EPS_t) = a + b*t

methods_all.name: Linear Regression
methods_all.description: Estimate growth rate by regressing earnings per share against a time variable.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Earnings per share increased 9.52 cents a year from 1991 to 2000, converted into percent growth by dividing the change by average EPS over period.

methods_all.name: Log-Linear Regression
methods_all.description: Estimate compounded percent growth in earnings per share.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Coefficient on time variable represents compounded percent growth; GE's EPS grew at 13.35% a year based on this regression.

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate growth rate using higher of EPS_t or EPS_(t-1) in the denominator.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (EPS_t - EPS_(t-1)) / max(|EPS_t|, |EPS_(t-1)|)

methods_all.name: ARIMA model
methods_all.description: Analyzes and forecasts univariate time series data.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: p = Degree of the autoregressive part, d = Degree of differencing, q = Degree of the moving average process. Model: w_t = φ_1w_{t-1} + ... + φ_pw_{t-p} - θ_1ε_{t-1} - ... - θ_qε_{t-q} + ε_t

methods_all.name: time series models
methods_all.description: predicting earnings per share using historical data
methods_all.rules_or_formula: requires a lot of data, typically quarterly earnings; model parameters may not be stationary over time

methods_all.name: Correlation Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing the correlation between growth rates of earnings over different time periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculate correlations for one-, three-, and five-year periods to assess predictability.

methods_all.name: Earnings vs Revenue Growth
methods_all.description: Analyzing historical growth rates of revenues versus earnings for forecasting.

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyzing historical growth rates to predict future performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use geometric average for long-term growth rate estimation.

methods_all.name: Revenue Growth Focus
methods_all.description: Use revenue growth as a more stable indicator for future projections.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None provided, but emphasis on using revenue over earnings due to stability.

methods_all.name: Year-by-Year Analysis
methods_all.description: Analyze annual growth rates rather than averages to understand changes in firm dynamics.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None provided, but suggests looking at yearly data for better projections.

methods_all.name: Near-term Projections
methods_all.description: Use historical growth rates only for near-future (next 1-2 years) due to rapid technological change.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: None provided, but advises caution beyond short term forecasts.

methods_all.name: Analyst Growth Estimates
methods_all.description: Uses analyst forecasts to predict future earnings and growth rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Incorporate analyst estimates while considering market capitalization, institutional holding, trading volume.

methods_all.name: Firm-specific information
methods_all.description: Use recent public information about the firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Reevaluate expected cash flows based on new data.

methods_all.name: Macroeconomic information
methods_all.description: Consider economic news and policy changes.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust growth projections for firms based on GNP, interest rates, inflation, fiscal and monetary policies.

methods_all.name: Competitor information
methods_all.description: Use competitor's future prospects to inform estimates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Reassess earnings forecasts of similar firms when competitors report negative results.

methods_all.name: Private Information Utilization
methods_all.description: Using non-public data for forecasting.

methods_all.name: Earnings Forecasting Models
methods_all.description: Models that incorporate various financial variables for predicting future earnings.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use of variables such as earnings retention, profit margins, and asset turnover in forecasting models.

methods_all.name: Analyst Forecast Weighting
methods_all.description: Determines how much weight to give analyst forecasts when estimating future growth.

methods_all.name: Weighting Analyst Forecasts
methods_all.description: Determine the weight assigned to analyst forecasts based on four factors.

methods_all.name: Forecast Error Measure
methods_all.description: Measuring the quality of analyst forecasts relative to historical data models.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: The smaller the forecast error, the higher the weight given to analyst forecasts.

methods_all.name: Stock Price Effect Measure
methods_all.description: Assessing the impact of analyst revisions on stock prices as a measure of forecast informativeness.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Greater effect on stock prices indicates more informative forecasts.

methods_all.name: Analyst Forecast Weighting
methods_all.description: Isolating and weighting forecasts from analysts who are better at predicting earnings.

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates earnings per share and net income growth based on ROE and retention ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: g = b * ROE, where g is the growth rate, b is the retention ratio, and ROE is return on equity.

methods_all.name: Return on Equity (ROE) formulation
methods_all.description: Calculates ROE based on book value and leverage.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: ROC = EBIT(1 - t)/(BV of debt + BV of equity-Cash), D/E = BV of debt/BV of equity, i = Interest expense on debt/BV of debt

methods_all.name: Marginal Return on Equity
methods_all.description: Measures the returns from new investments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Marginal return on equity = Δ Net income / Δ Book value of equity

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates expected growth in earnings per share based on changes in return on equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Growth Rate = Return on Equity × Retention Ratio

methods_all.name: Reinvestment Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate future reinvestment rate using historical data or industry averages.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Consider average reinvestment rates over time for firms with few large projects; use industry averages for firms that have recently expanded significantly.

methods_all.name: Return on Capital Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjust return on capital to reflect true returns on future investments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use trends in current return on capital and industry average return on capital to forecast future returns. Adjust for book value inaccuracies and competitive dynamics.

methods_all.name: Measuring the Reinvestment Rate, Return on Capital, and Expected Growth Rate
methods_all.description: Estimates reinvestment rate and return on capital to determine expected growth rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Growth Rate = Reinvestment Rate * Return on Capital

methods_all.name: reinvestment rate calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates growth based on reinvestment and return on capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth Rate = Reinvestment Rate * Return on Capital

methods_all.name: Reinvestment Rate and Return on Capital
methods_all.description: Calculates sustainable growth rate using reinvestment rates and returns on capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth rate = Reinvestment rate * Return on Capital

methods_all.name: Calculating Expected Growth
methods_all.description: Estimate expected growth using the marginal return on capital and reinvestment rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (marginal return on capital * reinvestment rate)

methods_all.name: Estimating Expected Growth Rate
methods_all.description: Calculate the growth rate in operating income for a firm with changing return on capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use a spreadsheet like chgrowht.xls to input varying rates of return on capital over time and estimate future growth in operating income.

methods_all.name: Negative Return on Capital Scenario Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate growth in revenues first, then use expected operating margin to estimate future operating income.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If the firm is losing money and has a negative return on capital, project revenue growth before estimating operating income.

methods_all.name: Revenue Growth Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating future revenue growth based on past performance and scaling effects.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth rates decline as revenues scale up, approaching the economy's growth rate by year 10.

methods_all.name: Estimating Operating Margins
methods_all.description: Use historical data from established firms to estimate future margins for new companies.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Assume greater marginal improvements in earlier years and smaller ones as the firm approaches maturity.

methods_all.name: Market Share Backward Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate future market share and reverse engineer required growth rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Revenue = Current Market Size * Future Market Share

methods_all.name: Historical Growth Projection
methods_all.description: Forecast near-term revenue growth based on historical data, then use industry averages for longer term projections.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Future Revenue = Current Revenue * (1 + Near-Term Growth Rate)^n

methods_all.name: Sales-to-Capital Ratio
methods_all.description: Estimate reinvestment needs by dividing projected revenue growth by the sales-to-capital ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Reinvestment Need = Projected Revenue Growth / Sales-to-Capital Ratio

methods_all.name: Imputed Return on Capital
methods_all.description: Calculate after-tax return on capital to check if reinvestment needs are accurately estimated.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Return on Capital = Estimated After-Tax Operating Income / Total Capital Invested

methods_all.name: Sales-to-Capital Ratio Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate sales-to-capital ratio for firms to determine investment needs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use industry average or marginal sales-to-capital ratio, adjusted for firm-specific factors.

methods_all.name: Quantitative Structure for Growth Estimates
methods_all.description: A method to translate qualitative factors into quantitative elements of growth such as returns on capital, margins, and reinvestment rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Not explicitly provided but implied through examples like management quality affecting return on capital estimates.

methods_all.name: Historical Growth Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating future growth based on past performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use historical EPS data to calculate arithmetic and geometric average growth rates.

methods_all.name: Analyst Estimates of Growth
methods_all.description: Using analysts' forecasts for firm growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Consider the limitations of analyst estimates compared to historical data.

methods_all.name: Fundamental-Based Growth Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimating future growth based on a firm's financial fundamentals.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use ROE, retention ratios, and reinvestment rates to estimate EPS growth.

methods_all.name: Earnings Per Share Growth Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the expected growth rate in earnings per share based on return on equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EPS Growth Rate = Return on Equity * Retention Ratio

methods_all.name: Equity Reinvestment Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate the equity reinvestment rate and estimate the expected growth in net income.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Reinvestment Rate = (Capital Expenditures + Increase in Working Capital - Debt Issuance) / Net Income

methods_all.name: Expected growth rate calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates annual expected growth rate from compounded growth over multiple years.

methods_all.name: Margin computation for Linkedin
methods_all.description: Averages current margin with target margin to estimate future margins.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Margin this year + Target margin)/2

methods_all.name: Margin computation for Tesla Motors
methods_all.description: Subtracts target margin from current margin and divides by 3 to estimate future margins.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Margin this year – Target margin)/3

methods_all.name: Margin Calculation for LinkedIn
methods_all.description: Calculates margin as average of current and target margins.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Margin this year + Target margin)/2

methods_all.name: Margin Calculation for Tesla Motors
methods_all.description: Calculates margin as difference between current and target margins divided by three.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Margin this year – Target margin)/3

methods_all.name: Perpetual Growth Model
methods_all.description: Estimates terminal value assuming constant growth rate forever.

methods_all.name: Liquidation Value Based on Book Value
methods_all.description: Adjusts book value of assets for inflation to estimate liquidation value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Book Value * (1 + Inflation Rate)^Years)

methods_all.name: Earning Power Liquidation Value
methods_all.description: Estimates cash flows from assets and discounts them back to present value using cost of capital.

methods_all.name: Multiple Approach
methods_all.description: Applies a multiple (e.g., price-to-sales) to future earnings or revenues to estimate terminal value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Future Earnings/Revenues * Multiple)

methods_all.name: Stable Growth Model
methods_all.description: Estimates terminal value assuming constant growth forever.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Terminal Value = (FCFE * (1 + g)) / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Limitation
methods_all.description: Determining stable growth rates based on economic constraints and valuation terms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Stable growth rate <= overall economy's growth rate; nominal vs real valuation impacts the limit.

methods_all.name: Stable Growth Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the stable growth rate by adding expected inflation to real growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: SGR = Real Growth + Expected Inflation

methods_all.name: Sustainable Growth Analysis
methods_all.description: Evaluating when a firm transitions from high growth to stable growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: High growth firms will eventually face barriers due to size or competition, leading to stable growth.

methods_all.name: Momentum Investing
methods_all.description: Firms with rapidly growing revenues and high excess returns are likely to sustain these trends.

methods_all.name: Competitive Advantage Period (CAP)
methods_all.description: Measures the period during which a firm can be expected to earn excess returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of such a firm = capital invested today + present value of future excess returns

methods_all.name: Estimating Length of High Growth Period
methods_all.description: Involves subjective judgments about how long a company can maintain high growth.

methods_all.name: Stable Growth Beta Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjust beta for firms in stable growth to ensure it does not exceed 1.2.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta <= 1.2

methods_all.name: Commodity Company Beta Adjustment
methods_all.description: If a commodity company is expected to diversify, adjust its beta upward towards 1 but should not be lower than 0.8.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta >= 0.8

methods_all.name: Adjusting Beta for Business Diversification
methods_all.description: If a company is expected to diversify into other businesses, adjust its beta upward.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: For stable period betas, ensure they are not lower than 8.

methods_all.name: industry excess returns
methods_all.description: Estimating value based on industry averages of ROE and ROC.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Assume a firm's ROE and ROC will converge to the industry average over time.

methods_all.name: Debt Ratio Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusts the debt ratio based on managers' views and stockholders' power.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If managers are willing to change financing policy and stockholders retain some power, assume a higher debt ratio in stable growth; otherwise, maintain current levels.

methods_all.name: Reestimate Rating
methods_all.description: Reevaluate the firm's rating as earnings and cash flows increase.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Internal consistency requires reestimating ratings and cost of debt when changing revenues and operating income forecasts.

methods_all.name: Debt Ratio and Cost of Debt Estimation
methods_all.description: Use industry average debt ratio and cost of debt for firms in stable growth.

methods_all.name: Retention Ratio Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate retention ratio as a function of expected growth rate and return on equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Retention Ratio = (Expected Growth Rate / Return on Equity)

methods_all.name: Equity Reinvestment Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Compute equity reinvestment rate based on stable growth rate and return on equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Equity Reinvestment Rate = (Expected Growth Rate / Return on Equity)

methods_all.name: Equity Reinvestment Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates reinvestment rate based on stable growth and return on equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: reinvestment rate = (stable growth rate / return on equity) * 100%

methods_all.name: Reinvestment Rate in Stable Growth
methods_all.description: Determines the reinvestment rate using ROC and stable growth assumptions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: reinvestment rate = (ROC * stable growth rate) / (return on capital - cost of capital)

methods_all.name: Return on Equity vs Cost of Equity
methods_all.description: Analyzing the relationship between return on equity and cost of equity in stable growth scenarios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: A return on equity equal to the cost of equity nullifies the positive effect of growth.

methods_all.name: Terminal Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the value of a firm based on expected future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: TV = CF_{n+1} / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Valuation method using expected dividends and discount rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of future dividends discounted at the cost of equity.

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity Model
methods_all.description: Estimates intrinsic value based on cash flows to shareholders.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discounted free cash flow to equity using weighted average cost of capital.

methods_all.name: Two-Stage Valuation Model
methods_all.description: Uses a high-growth phase followed by a stable growth phase.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth rate transitions from high to stable in one year.

methods_all.name: N-Stage Valuation Model
methods_all.description: Allows for gradual changes in each year, suitable for firms with very high or negative operating margins.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Gradual adjustment of growth rates and risk characteristics over multiple stages.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing a firm by discounting expected future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Terminal value assumes perpetual life; survival risk can be incorporated into cost of capital.

methods_all.name: Discounting for Survival Risk
methods_all.description: Adjusts valuation based on probability of distress.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Value = DCF Value - (Probability of Distress * (DCF Value - Distressed Sale Value))

methods_all.name: Terminal Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate future cash flows and apply a growth rate to determine value beyond explicit forecast period.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: TV = CFn * (1 + g) / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Terminal Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate terminal value based on growth rates and cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: TV = CF / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Valuing equity by calculating the present value of expected dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(DPS_t / (1 + k_e)^t) for t=1 to infinity

methods_all.name: Gordon Growth Model
methods_all.description: Values a firm in steady state with dividends growing at a sustainable rate forever.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Gordon Growth Model
methods_all.description: Calculates stock value based on expected dividends and growth rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Gordon Growth Model
methods_all.description: Valuation method for firms with stable growth and consistent dividend payout policies.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Calculates the intrinsic value of a stock based on the present value of future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Values a stock based on the present value of all future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Two-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Allows for two stages of growth, an initial phase and a subsequent steady state.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: DPS_t = Expected dividends per share in year t; k_e = Cost of equity (hg: high growth period; st: stable growth period); P_n = Price at the end of year n; g = Extraordinary growth rate for the first n years; g_n = Growth rate forever after year n

methods_all.name: Two-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A model that values firms with high initial growth followed by stable growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Algebraic manipulation yields the payout ratio for stable period: (1 - g / ROE) * 100%, where g is the growth rate and ROE is return on equity.

methods_all.name: Two-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Valuation method that accounts for high growth phase followed by stable growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = PV(high growth dividends) + PV(stable growth terminal value)

methods_all.name: Two-Stage Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Valuation method using expected dividends during high growth and stable periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of future dividends discounted at cost of equity.

methods_all.name: Augmented Payout Ratio
methods_all.description: Incorporates stock buybacks into the dividend discount model.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Add stock buybacks to dividends and compute an augmented payout ratio. Use a four- or five-year average for better estimates.

methods_all.name: Augmented Dividend Payout Ratio
methods_all.description: Calculates total cash returned to stockholders including dividends and stock buybacks.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Augmented Payout Ratio = (Dividends + Stock Buybacks) / Net Income

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Estimates stock value based on future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Calculates intrinsic value based on future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = (DPS_t / (k_e - g_n)) + Value of stable growth

methods_all.name: H Model for Valuing Growth
methods_all.description: A two-stage model where initial growth declines linearly to a stable rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P0 = Σ(DPS_t / (k_e - g_t)), where g_t is the declining growth rate over time.

methods_all.name: H Model
methods_all.description: Valuation model that accounts for a transition from high growth to stable growth with linearly decreasing growth rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = (D1 / (r - g)) + ((g0 - g) * D1 / (r - g)^2), where D1 is the next period's dividend, r is the required rate of return, g0 is initial growth rate, and g is stable growth rate.

methods_all.name: H model
methods_all.description: Valuation method combining high and stable growth phases.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses expected dividends during different growth stages.

methods_all.name: Three-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Combines initial high growth, transitional declining growth, and final stable low growth periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Does not impose restrictions on payout ratio.

methods_all.name: Three-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Calculates stock value based on present value of dividends during high-growth and transitional periods, plus terminal price at start of stable growth phase.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = PV(Dividends_high_growth + Dividends_transitional) + Terminal_Price

methods_all.name: Three-Stage Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A valuation model that divides the company's life into three stages: high growth, transition to stable growth, and stable growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of dividends during each stage is calculated using respective cost of equity and growth rates.

methods_all.name: Three-Stage Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Values a firm with high growth, transition to stable growth periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Calculates dividends and present values for each stage, adjusting cost of equity linearly from high to stable growth.

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A valuation model that discounts future dividends to present value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Valuing stocks based on expected future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A valuation model used to estimate the value of a stock based on its future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the intrinsic value of a stock based on expected future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: A valuation method used to determine the intrinsic value of a stock based on its future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Calculates intrinsic value of a stock based on future dividend payments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Steady-state payout ratio
methods_all.description: Using a steady-state payout ratio for firms with little or no dividends.

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity Discount Model
methods_all.description: A valuation model that considers cash flows left over after meeting all financial obligations.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFE = Net Income - Reinvestment Needs (Capital Expenditures, Working Capital Changes) + Depreciation and Amortization

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE)
methods_all.description: Calculates the cash flows available for equity investors after meeting capital expenditure and working capital needs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFE = Net Income - Dividends + Depreciation & Amortization - Capital Expenditures - Changes in Working Capital + (δ * (Capital Expenditures + Changes in Working Capital))

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE) Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusts for preferred stock and dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Subtract preferred dividends from total cash flow to arrive at FCFE.

methods_all.name: Modified Dividend Payout Ratio
methods_all.description: Measures the total cash returned to stockholders as a proportion of free cash flow to equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cash to Stockholders / FCFE

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusts for the buildup of cash when firms pay out less than they can afford.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If payout is less, adjust valuation upwards; if payout exceeds available funds, adjust downwards.

methods_all.name: FCFE Valuation Models
methods_all.description: Discount potential dividends rather than actual dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE) = Operating Cash Flow - Capital Expenditures + Net Borrowing

methods_all.name: FCFE valuation model
methods_all.description: A variant of the dividend discount model where free cash flows to equity replace dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discounting FCFE involves assuming that all available cash after debt payments and reinvestment needs is paid out to stockholders.

methods_all.name: Estimating Growth in FCFE
methods_all.description: Uses a modified approach compared to dividend growth estimation, replacing the retention ratio with the equity reinvestment rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth in FCFE = Equity Reinvestment Rate * Modified Return on Equity

methods_all.name: Modified ROE
methods_all.description: Measures expected growth rate in FCFE.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected Growth Rate = Equity Reinvestment Rate * Modified ROE

methods_all.name: Constant Growth FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Values firms growing at a stable rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = (FCFE1 / (ke - gn))

methods_all.name: Stable Growth FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Valuation method for stable firms that pay out dividends based on Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE).
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = FCFE / (Cost of Equity - Expected Growth Rate)

methods_all.name: FCFE computation
methods_all.description: Calculates free cash flow to equity considering the firm's debt ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Increasing leverage can increase FCFE but also increases cost of equity due to higher beta.

methods_all.name: levered beta equation
methods_all.description: Calculates levered beta.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Levered Beta = Unlevered Beta * (1 + (1 - Tax Rate) * (Debt/Equity))

methods_all.name: constant growth FCFE model troubleshooting guide
methods_all.description: Provides solutions for common issues in valuation using the constant growth FCFE model.

methods_all.name: two-stage FCFE model
methods_all.description: Valuation method for firms expected to grow faster initially and then stabilize.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = PV of FCFE during high growth period + Terminal Price

methods_all.name: Fundamental Growth Equation
methods_all.description: Calculates equity reinvestment rate based on growth and ROE.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Equity Reinvestment Rate = g/ROE

methods_all.name: Two-Stage FCFE Model
methods_all.description: A valuation model that considers high and stable growth periods separately.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Estimate cost of equity, calculate free cash flows to equity (FCFE), apply different growth rates for each period.

methods_all.name: Present Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of FCFE during high-growth and stable growth periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: PV = FCFE / (1 + r)^t

methods_all.name: FCFE valuation method
methods_all.description: Valuing a firm with high growth period followed by stable growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses spreadsheet FCFE2st.xls to calculate terminal value and equity value.

methods_all.name: Three-Stage FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of expected free cash flow to equity over three stages of growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: P0 = ∑(FCFEt / (1 + ke)^t) + Pn2 / (1 + ke)^(n2+1), where FCFE is Free Cash Flow to Equity, ke is Cost of Equity, and g is the stable growth rate.

methods_all.name: Two-Stage FCFE Model Troubleshooting
methods_all.description: Identifies and resolves issues with extremely low or high valuation results.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust earnings, capital expenditures, beta, working capital ratio, and growth rate as specified.

methods_all.name: Reinvestment Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate reinvestment rate for high growth firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: g/ROE

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate FCFE for high-growth and transition periods using growth rates and reinvestment rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFE = Net Income * (1 - Reinvestment Rate) + (Net Income * Reinvestment Rate * Return on Equity)

methods_all.name: Three-Stage FCFE Model
methods_all.description: A valuation method that considers high-growth, transition, and stable growth periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Sum up the present value of FCFE over the high growth period and add to it the present value of the terminal value of equity.

methods_all.name: Growth Rate Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjust the growth rate for high-growth firms to be lower than or equal to GNP growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Growth Rate <= GNP Growth

methods_all.name: FCFE Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing a firm with high growth in FCFE, followed by transition and stable periods.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses discounted cash flow model with Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE).

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Alternative valuation method focusing on dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discounted sum of future dividends paid out to shareholders.

methods_all.name: FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the Free Cash Flow to Equity, reflecting noncash investments.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Measures growth only in income from operating assets.

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model (DDM)
methods_all.description: Assumes dividends are paid and remaining earnings are reinvested.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Measures growth in income from both operating and cash assets.

methods_all.name: Three-Stage FCFE Model
methods_all.description: A valuation method that separates after-tax interest income and computes noncash net income, then uses equity reinvestment rate (ERR) to estimate growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Noncash ROE = Net Income - After-Tax Interest Income / Book Value of Equity - Cash; ERR = (Net Capex + Working Capital Investment) / Book Value of Equity

methods_all.name: FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the value of a company's equity based on free cash flow to equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present value of FCFE over next 10 years + terminal value discounted back at cumulated cost of equity

methods_all.name: Dividend Discount Model
methods_all.description: Estimates the value of a stock based on future dividends.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: V = D1 / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity Model
methods_all.description: Estimates the intrinsic value of equity by discounting expected free cash flows to equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFE = NI + Depreciation - Capital Expenditures - Change in Net Working Capital - Tax Shield on New Debt

methods_all.name: FCFE Model
methods_all.description: Estimates value per share based on free cash flow to equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Sum of discounted FCFE + Terminal Value

methods_all.name: Equity Reinvestment Rate
methods_all.description: Estimate the reinvestment rate of equity.

methods_all.name: Return on Equity (ROE)
methods_all.description: Calculate ROE based on given financial data.

methods_all.name: Expected Growth Rate
methods_all.description: Determine expected growth rate for a firm.

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE)
methods_all.description: Estimate FCFE using projected free cash flows.

methods_all.name: Free Cash Flow to Firm (FCFF)
methods_all.description: Sum of cash flows to all claim holders in the firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFF = EBIT(1-T) - Net Investment

methods_all.name: FCFF
methods_all.description: Free cash flow to firm calculation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discounting FCFF at the cost of capital yields the value of operating assets plus nonoperating assets.

methods_all.name: FCFE
methods_all.description: Free cash flow to equity calculation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discounting FCFE at the cost of equity yields the value of equity in a business.

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Approach
methods_all.description: Value a firm by discounting free cash flow at WACC.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFF1 / (WACC - g_n)

methods_all.name: Reinvestment Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Derive reinvestment rate from stable growth rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: reinvestment rate = growth rate / return on capital

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate cost of capital for a stable growth firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: cost of equity = risk-free rate + beta * (equity risk premium - mature market premium - country risk premium)

methods_all.name: Cost of Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Incorporates country risk premium, cost of debt, and debt ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Equity = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * Market Risk Premium + Country Risk Premium

methods_all.name: FCFF Model
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of expected free cash flows to the firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = ∑(t=1 to n) (FCFF_t / (WACC)^t)

methods_all.name: Iterative Cost of Capital Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the cost of capital using market value weights and then reestimate with estimated values to converge on a solution.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Substitute back the estimated value and iterate until convergence.

methods_all.name: Two-Stage FCFF Model
methods_all.description: A valuation method that uses free cash flow to the firm over two stages of growth.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: First stage: high growth period with a specific growth rate. Second stage: stable growth period with a lower, constant growth rate.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis
methods_all.description: Calculates the present value of future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Present Value = Future Cash Flows / (1 + Cost of Capital)^n

methods_all.name: Capitalizing R&D
methods_all.description: Estimating the value impact of capitalizing R&D expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Using a 10-year amortizable life for R&D and adjusting operating income and return on capital.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis
methods_all.description: Estimating the value of equity per share by discounting projected cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t), where CFt is the cash flow in year t, and r is the cost of capital.

methods_all.name: Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates levered beta for Gerdau Steel using unlevered beta and debt-to-equity ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Levered Beta = Unlevered Beta * (1 + (1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity)

methods_all.name: Revenue-based approach
methods_all.description: Dividing Gerdau's Brazilian revenue proportion by the average revenue proportion for a Brazilian company to yield lambda.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Lambda = (Gerdau's Brazilian Revenue Proportion) / (Average Revenue Proportion of Brazilian Company)

methods_all.name: Price-based approach
methods_all.description: Regressing weekly returns on Gerdau stock against Brazilian government dollar-denominated bond yields to estimate lambda and cost of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Lambda = Regression coefficient from weekly returns; Cost of Equity = 17.61% (example value provided)

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis
methods_all.description: Estimating future cash flows and discounting them at a cost of capital to determine the intrinsic value of an asset.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Intrinsic Value = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t), where CFt is the expected cash flow in year t, and r is the discount rate.

methods_all.name: Adjusting cost of capital for inflation
methods_all.description: Adjust the stable period cost of capital to reflect higher inflation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: BR cost of capital = Stable period cost of capital + Inflation adjustment factor

methods_all.name: Discounting cash flows at high growth rate
methods_all.description: Calculate terminal value by discounting BR cash flows back at a high growth period cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Terminal Value = Cash Flows / (Cost of Capital - Growth Rate)

methods_all.name: FCFF approach
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of a firm using Free Cash Flow to Firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Spreadsheet provided for FCFF valuation.

methods_all.name: FCFF valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing a firm using Free Cash Flow to the Firm (FCFF) method.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of equity = Value of firm - Market value of outstanding debt

methods_all.name: Operating Income to Net Income Calculation
methods_all.description: Subtract interest expenses and taxes from operating income.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Net Income = Operating Income - Interest Expenses - Taxes

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) Approach
methods_all.description: Estimates the value of a leveraged firm by adding the present value of interest tax savings and subtracting the expected cost of bankruptcy to the value of an unlevered firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = V_U + PV(Tax Shield) - Expected Cost of Bankruptcy

methods_all.name: Unlevered Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the unlevered beta of a firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: β_unlevered = β_current / (1 + (1 - t) * D/E)

methods_all.name: Expected Tax Benefit from Borrowing
methods_all.description: Estimates the tax benefit from debt based on interest payments and tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Tax Benefit = Interest Payments * Tax Rate

methods_all.name: Present Value of Bankruptcy Costs
methods_all.description: Evaluates the expected bankruptcy costs with additional debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: PV(Bankruptcy Cost) = π_a * BC

methods_all.name: APV approach
methods_all.description: Valuing a company by first valuing it as an all-equity funded firm and then adding the value of debt tax shields.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of levered firm = Value of unlevered firm + Present value of tax shield from debt

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) Valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates the value of a levered firm by adding the net effect of debt to the unlevered firm value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Unlevered Firm Value + Net Effect of Debt

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Approach
methods_all.description: Incorporates the effects of leverage in the cost of capital, with tax benefits and bankruptcy costs included.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Unlevered Firm Value * (1 + Leverage Effects)

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) approach
methods_all.description: A valuation method that incorporates the tax benefits from debt.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of levered firm = Unlevered firm value + PV of tax shields

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Approach
methods_all.description: Calculates cost of equity, after-tax cost of debt, and weights on debt and equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Levered beta = Unlevered beta * (1 + (1 - tax rate) * (debt/equity))

methods_all.name: Levered Beta Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate levered beta as a function of market debt to equity ratio, unlevered beta, and firm's marginal tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: levered_beta = unlevered_beta * (1 + (1 - tax_rate) * (debt/equity))

methods_all.name: Cost of Debt Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate cost of debt using bond ratings as measure of default risk and applying a default spread to the risk-free rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: cost_of_debt = risk_free_rate + default_spread * (1 - tax_rate)

methods_all.name: Cost of Equity Calculation
methods_all.description: Uses unlevered beta, Treasury bond rate, and equity risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Equity = Risk-Free Rate + (Unlevered Beta * Market Premium)

methods_all.name: Interest Coverage Ratio Method
methods_all.description: Links rating to interest coverage ratio.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Rating and default spread are determined by the firm's interest coverage ratio.

methods_all.name: Debt Ratio Assessment
methods_all.description: Calculates optimal debt ratio for a company based on EBIT and interest expenses.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: EBIT / Interest Expense = Interest Coverage Ratio; Rating is derived from the coverage ratio.

methods_all.name: Synthetic Rating Calculation
methods_all.description: Determines synthetic bond ratings at different debt levels using interest coverage ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Interest Coverage Ratio -> Synthetic Bond Rating -> Default Spread (from Table 8.2) + Risk-Free Rate = Pretax Cost of Debt

methods_all.name: Debt Refinancing Method
methods_all.description: Calculates after-tax costs of debt at different levels.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Determine the dollar value of debt by multiplying the debt ratio by the firm's market value and adjust for refinancing at new interest rates based on credit ratings.

methods_all.name: Debt Ratio Optimization
methods_all.description: Determines the optimal debt ratio to minimize cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of Capital = f(D/E) where D is Debt and E is Equity. Optimal when Cost of Capital reaches minimum.

methods_all.name: Implied Growth Rate Calculation
methods_all.description: Back out an implied growth rate from the firm's market value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Firm = Market Value of Equity + Market Value of Debt

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Adjustment
methods_all.description: Estimate change in firm value based on a drop in cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Savings = Change in Cost of Capital * Annual Financing Costs

methods_all.name: Firm Value Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate firm value at different debt ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use the capped growth rate of 0.68% to estimate firm value.

methods_all.name: Optimal Debt Ratio Calculation
methods_all.description: Computes optimal debt ratio for firm value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses updated interest coverage ratios and default spreads.

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) Approach
methods_all.description: Estimates the value of a levered firm by considering both benefits and costs of borrowing.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Firm Value = Market Value - Tax Benefits + Bankruptcy Costs

methods_all.name: APV Approach
methods_all.description: Estimates optimal capital structure by maximizing value of levered firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of unlevered firm + PV of tax savings - expected bankruptcy costs

methods_all.name: Tax Savings Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate tax savings at different levels of debt, adjusting tax rate if interest expenses exceed EBIT.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust tax rate in calculation if interest expenses > EBIT.

methods_all.name: Bankruptcy Cost Estimation
methods_all.description: Calculate expected bankruptcy cost based on bond ratings and probabilities of default.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Expected bankruptcy cost = 25% of firm value at a given debt ratio.

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value (APV) Approach
methods_all.description: A method to compute the value of a levered firm by valuing debt and operating assets separately.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = Value of Operating Assets + Value of Debt

methods_all.name: APV approach
methods_all.description: An alternative valuation method that considers the effect of debt on firm value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Firm Value + (Tax Benefits - Bankruptcy Costs)

methods_all.name: FCFF approach
methods_all.description: Estimate free cash flow to the firm and use it to calculate firm and equity values.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: FCFF = EBIT * (1 - Tax Rate) + Depreciation - Capital Expenditures - Change in Working Capital

methods_all.name: Cost of Capital Estimation
methods_all.description: Calculates the cost of capital for a company based on its debt and equity structure.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: WACC = (E/V) * Re + (D/V) * Rd * (1-Tc)

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value Approach
methods_all.description: Estimates firm value considering tax shields, costs of bankruptcy, and probabilities of default.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: APV = PV(Unlevered Firm) + PV(Tax Shields) - PV(Costs of Bankruptcy)

methods_all.name: Adjusted Present Value Approach
methods_all.description: Estimates levered value of a firm considering debt and equity costs.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Cost of capital = (Cost of equity * Debt ratio) + (Cost of debt * Equity ratio)

methods_all.name: beta_of_debt_calculation
methods_all.description: Calculating the beta of debt using default spread and market risk premium.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Beta = (Default Spread * 25%) / Market Risk Premium

methods_all.name: Estimating Equity Value per Share
methods_all.description: Calculating equity value by considering nonoperating assets and netting out nonequity claims.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Firm Value - Nonequity Claims

methods_all.name: Consolidated Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing total assets including financial investments as a whole.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjust cost of equity or capital for the proportion of cash and marketable securities.

methods_all.name: Separate Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing operating assets and non-operating assets (cash, marketable securities) individually.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use separate discount rates based on risk profiles of different asset types.

methods_all.name: Separate Valuation Method
methods_all.description: Valuing operating assets and financial assets separately.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Back out income from financial assets before estimating free cash flows to the firm or equity.

methods_all.name: Discounting Cash
methods_all.description: Calculating the value of cash based on its actual investment rate versus market rates.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of cash = Cash amount / (1 + discount rate)^n

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow with Probability Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusts the value of cash based on probability and expected net present value of poor acquisitions.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Original Value - (Probability * Overpayment) - Discount for Management Quality

methods_all.name: Risk-Return Tradeoff
methods_all.description: Investing in riskier securities to earn higher returns.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Higher risk securities (corporate bonds, high-yield bonds, equities) yield higher expected returns compared to lower risk securities (Treasury bills, commercial paper).

methods_all.name: Undervalued Securities
methods_all.description: Investing in stocks and bonds that are undervalued by the market.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Firms should seek investments where the return exceeds the cost of equity or debt.

methods_all.name: Strategic Investments
methods_all.description: Investing in equity of other firms for strategic benefits.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Depends on the value created through side benefits and synergies compared to investment cost.

methods_all.name: Market Value Approach
methods_all.description: Estimate current market value of marketable securities.

methods_all.name: Capital Gains Tax Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjust market value for capital gains taxes based on purchase price and current value.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Approach
methods_all.description: Value firms issuing the securities using a discounted cash flow method.

methods_all.name: Market Value Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusting market value of securities to reflect overvaluation or undervaluation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If investments are overvalued, discount them; if they deliver higher returns than expected, consider a premium.

methods_all.name: Discount Calculation for Underperforming Funds
methods_all.description: Calculates expected discount based on underperformance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: On a percent basis, the discount represents 16.67% of the market value of the investments.

methods_all.name: Marking to Market
methods_all.description: Revalues trading investments at market levels.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Market value trumps book value for trading assets.

methods_all.name: Equity Approach
methods_all.description: Adjusts acquisition cost based on net income and losses of the invested firm.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Acquisition cost adjusted by proportional share of net income/losses and dividends received.

methods_all.name: Valuation of minority holdings
methods_all.description: Value equity in each firm, take percent share, and add to parent company's value.

methods_all.name: Consolidation adjustment
methods_all.description: Strip subsidiary income, assets, and debt from parent before consolidation.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing equity in operating assets, considering growth rates and cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value = Ω / (r - g)

methods_all.name: Valuing Cross Holdings
methods_all.description: Estimate market values of cross holdings using book values and price-to-book ratios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: For publicly traded firms, use the market value. For private businesses, apply the P/B ratio of similar public companies.

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) with Cross Holdings Adjustment
methods_all.description: Adjusts DCF valuation by adding market value of cross holdings.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Market Value = Sum(DCF Valuation + Market Value of Cross Holdings)

methods_all.name: valuation of unutilized land
methods_all.description: Estimate market value or project cash flows and discount them.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Market Value = Current Market Price OR Discounted Cash Flows

methods_all.name: Firm Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate firm value by summing operating assets, cash and marketable securities, and other nonoperating assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = Operating Assets + Cash & Marketable Securities + Other Nonoperating Assets

methods_all.name: Equity Value Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate equity value by subtracting nonequity claims from firm value.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Firm Value - Nonequity Claims

methods_all.name: Expected Liability Calculation
methods_all.description: Estimate the expected liability from potential lawsuits.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Probability of losing case × Expected damages

methods_all.name: Option Overhang Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculates the reduction in equity value due to existing and expected future option grants.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Firm Value - Debt - Option Overhang

methods_all.name: Fully Diluted Number of Shares
methods_all.description: Divide equity value by fully diluted number of shares.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Equity Value / Fully Diluted Shares

methods_all.name: Expected Option Exercises
methods_all.description: Forecast future option exercises and build in expected dilution.

methods_all.name: Treasury Stock Approach
methods_all.description: Adjust shares for outstanding options and add expected proceeds to equity value.

methods_all.name: Value Options Using Option Pricing Model
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of options today given today’s value per share and time premium, then subtract from equity value and divide by shares outstanding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Equity Value - Estimated Option Value = Adjusted Equity Value; Adjusted Equity Value / Shares Outstanding = Value Per Share

methods_all.name: Option Valuation Method
methods_all.description: Estimate value per share by subtracting option value from equity value and dividing by shares outstanding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per Share = (Equity Value - Option Value) / Shares Outstanding

methods_all.name: Tax Benefit Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the tax benefit by multiplying the difference between stock price and exercise price by the tax rate.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: (Stock Price - Exercise Price) * Tax Rate

methods_all.name: After-Tax Value of Options
methods_all.description: Calculate after-tax value to consider potential tax benefits from option exercise in valuation.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Option Value * (1 - Tax Rate)

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation
methods_all.description: Calculates equity value by discounting future cash flows.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = Sum(Discounted Future Cash Flows)

methods_all.name: Valuation Adjustment for Voting Rights
methods_all.description: Adjust the value of equity per share based on voting rights.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per voting share = Value per non-voting share * (1 + premium percentage)

methods_all.name: Valuing Options with Dilution Effect
methods_all.description: Use spreadsheet to calculate value of options considering dilution.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: warrants.xls

methods_all.name: Valuation of voting versus nonvoting shares
methods_all.description: Estimate the value per share under status quo and optimal management scenarios.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: 1. Value firm under existing management policies; 2. Divide by total number of shares for non-voting share value; 3. Reassess firm value with better management policies; 4. Calculate control premium based on probability of management change.

methods_all.name: Fully Diluted Number of Shares Approach
methods_all.description: Divide the value of equity by fully diluted number of shares outstanding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Equity Value / Fully Diluted Shares Outstanding

methods_all.name: Treasury Stock Approach
methods_all.description: Add expected proceeds from option exercise to equity value and then divide by fully diluted number of shares outstanding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Equity Value = (Equity Value + Expected Proceeds) / Fully Diluted Shares Outstanding

methods_all.name: Option Pricing Model Approach
methods_all.description: Value options using an option pricing model, subtract from equity value, then divide by primary shares outstanding.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value per share = (Equity Value - Option Value) / Primary Shares Outstanding

methods_all.name: Consolidated Financial Statement Calculation
methods_all.description: Calculate consolidated earnings before interest and taxes for two companies.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Earnings Before Interest and Taxes (EBIT) = EBIT of Lava Lamps Inc. + EBIT of General Lamps Inc.

methods_all.name: Enterprise Value Estimation
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of equity in a company using financial metrics such as growth rate, cost of capital, tax rate, and return on capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = (EBIT * (1 - Tax Rate) / Cost of Capital) + Growth Rate

methods_all.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation
methods_all.description: Estimate the value of equity per share in a biotechnology firm using after-tax operating income, growth rate, reinvestment rate, and cost of capital.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = (After-Tax Operating Income * Reinvestment Rate) / (Cost of Capital - Growth Rate)

methods_all.name: Closed-End Fund Valuation
methods_all.description: Estimate the discount at which a closed-end fund would trade based on historical returns and expected future performance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Discount = Expected Return - Index Fund Return

methods_all.name: Treasury Stock Method for Options
methods_all.description: Calculate the correct value per share using the treasury stock approach, considering outstanding shares and options.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Correct Value Per Share = (Value of Equity + Exercise Price * Number of Options) / Total Shares Outstanding

methods_all.name: Riskless Rate Estimation
methods_all.description: Uses Treasury bills as risk-free proxy.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Use the yield on Treasury bills as a fair rate of return if cash is invested in similar assets.

methods_all.name: Unlevered Beta Calculation
methods_all.description: Adjusts beta for average cash balance.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Back out unlevered beta from regression considering constant cash balance ratio over period.

methods_all.name: Option Valuation with Forfeiture
methods_all.description: Valuing options considering forfeiture risk.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Iterative process using treasury stock approach to adjust option value and compute new share price.

methods_all.name: Relative Valuation
methods_all.description: Valuing assets based on market pricing of similar firms.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Uses multiples such as P/E, book values, or sales to standardize prices and compare with similar firms.

methods_all.name: Relative Valuation
methods_all.description: Evaluating a stock's value relative to its peers.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Compare multiples and comparable firms, but be cautious of market mood influence.

methods_all.name: PBV ratio
methods_all.description: Compares stock price to book value of equity.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Book Value (PBV) = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_all.name: Tobin's Q
methods_all.description: Ratio of firm value to replacement cost of assets.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Tobin's Q = Firm Value / Replacement Cost of Assets

methods_all.name: PS ratio
methods_all.description: Compares market value of equity to revenues.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Sales (PS) Ratio = Market Price per Share / Sales Revenue Per Share

methods_all.name: VS ratio
methods_all.description: Enterprise value scaled by revenue.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Value-to-Sales (VS) Ratio = Enterprise Value / Total Revenues

methods_all.name: FOUR BASIC STEPS TO USING MULTIPLES
methods_all.description: Guidelines for using multiples effectively.
methods_all.rules_or_formula:
1. Ensure consistency in defining and measuring the multiple across firms.
2. Be aware of cross-sectional distribution of the multiple within sectors and the entire market.
3. Understand how fundamentals affect the multiple.
4. Choose appropriate firms for comparison, controlling for persistent differences.

methods_all.name: consistent multiple definition
methods_all.description: Ensure numerator and denominator are of the same type.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: If equity value is used as numerator, use equity measure for denominator. If firm value is used as numerator, use firm measure for denominator.

methods_all.name: Descriptional Tests
methods_all.description: Identifying high, low, or typical values of a multiple in the market.
methods_all.rules_or_formula: Understand distributional characteristics and effects of outliers on averages.

methods_all.name: Adjusting Average PE Ratio
methods_all.description: Adjust the average price-earnings ratio to account for firms with negative earnings.
