methods_unique.description: Estimate market values of cross holdings using book values and price-to-book ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: For publicly traded firms, use the market value. For private businesses, apply the P/B ratio of similar public companies.

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) with Cross Holdings Adjustment
methods_unique.description: Adjusts DCF valuation by adding market value of cross holdings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Market Value = Sum(DCF Valuation + Market Value of Cross Holdings)

methods_unique.name: valuation of unutilized land
methods_unique.description: Estimate market value or project cash flows and discount them.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Market Value = Current Market Price OR Discounted Cash Flows

methods_unique.name: Firm Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimate firm value by summing operating assets, cash and marketable securities, and other nonoperating assets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = Operating Assets + Cash & Marketable Securities + Other Nonoperating Assets

methods_unique.name: Equity Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculate equity value by subtracting nonequity claims from firm value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Firm Value - Nonequity Claims

methods_unique.name: Expected Liability Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the expected liability from potential lawsuits.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Probability of losing case × Expected damages

methods_unique.name: Option Overhang Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the reduction in equity value due to existing and expected future option grants.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Firm Value - Debt - Option Overhang

methods_unique.name: Fully Diluted Number of Shares
methods_unique.description: Divide equity value by fully diluted number of shares.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Equity Value / Fully Diluted Shares

methods_unique.name: Expected Option Exercises
methods_unique.description: Forecast future option exercises and build in expected dilution.

methods_unique.name: Treasury Stock Approach
methods_unique.description: Adjust shares for outstanding options and add expected proceeds to equity value.

methods_unique.name: Value Options Using Option Pricing Model
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of options today given today’s value per share and time premium, then subtract from equity value and divide by shares outstanding.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Equity Value - Estimated Option Value = Adjusted Equity Value; Adjusted Equity Value / Shares Outstanding = Value Per Share

methods_unique.name: Option Valuation Method
methods_unique.description: Estimate value per share by subtracting option value from equity value and dividing by shares outstanding.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per Share = (Equity Value - Option Value) / Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Tax Benefit Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the tax benefit by multiplying the difference between stock price and exercise price by the tax rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Stock Price - Exercise Price) * Tax Rate

methods_unique.name: After-Tax Value of Options
methods_unique.description: Calculate after-tax value to consider potential tax benefits from option exercise in valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Option Value * (1 - Tax Rate)

methods_unique.name: Valuation Adjustment for Voting Rights
methods_unique.description: Adjust the value of equity per share based on voting rights.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per voting share = Value per non-voting share * (1 + premium percentage)

methods_unique.name: Valuing Options with Dilution Effect
methods_unique.description: Use spreadsheet to calculate value of options considering dilution.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: warrants.xls

methods_unique.name: Valuation of voting versus nonvoting shares
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value per share under status quo and optimal management scenarios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1. Value firm under existing management policies; 2. Divide by total number of shares for non-voting share value; 3. Reassess firm value with better management policies; 4. Calculate control premium based on probability of management change.

methods_unique.name: Fully Diluted Number of Shares Approach
methods_unique.description: Divide the value of equity by fully diluted number of shares outstanding.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Equity Value / Fully Diluted Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Option Pricing Model Approach
methods_unique.description: Value options using an option pricing model, subtract from equity value, then divide by primary shares outstanding.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = (Equity Value - Option Value) / Primary Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Consolidated Financial Statement Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculate consolidated earnings before interest and taxes for two companies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Earnings Before Interest and Taxes (EBIT) = EBIT of Lava Lamps Inc. + EBIT of General Lamps Inc.

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of equity in a company using financial metrics such as growth rate, cost of capital, tax rate, and return on capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = (EBIT * (1 - Tax Rate) / Cost of Capital) + Growth Rate

methods_unique.name: Closed-End Fund Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the discount at which a closed-end fund would trade based on historical returns and expected future performance.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Discount = Expected Return - Index Fund Return

methods_unique.name: Treasury Stock Method for Options
methods_unique.description: Calculate the correct value per share using the treasury stock approach, considering outstanding shares and options.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Correct Value Per Share = (Value of Equity + Exercise Price * Number of Options) / Total Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Option Valuation with Forfeiture
methods_unique.description: Valuing options considering forfeiture risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Iterative process using treasury stock approach to adjust option value and compute new share price.

methods_unique.name: PBV ratio
methods_unique.description: Compares stock price to book value of equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Book Value (PBV) = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_unique.name: Tobin's Q
methods_unique.description: Ratio of firm value to replacement cost of assets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Tobin's Q = Firm Value / Replacement Cost of Assets

methods_unique.name: PS ratio
methods_unique.description: Compares market value of equity to revenues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Sales (PS) Ratio = Market Price per Share / Sales Revenue Per Share

methods_unique.name: VS ratio
methods_unique.description: Enterprise value scaled by revenue.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value-to-Sales (VS) Ratio = Enterprise Value / Total Revenues

methods_unique.name: FOUR BASIC STEPS TO USING MULTIPLES
methods_unique.description: Guidelines for using multiples effectively.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula:
1. Ensure consistency in defining and measuring the multiple across firms.
2. Be aware of cross-sectional distribution of the multiple within sectors and the entire market.
3. Understand how fundamentals affect the multiple.
4. Choose appropriate firms for comparison, controlling for persistent differences.

methods_unique.name: consistent multiple definition
methods_unique.description: Ensure numerator and denominator are of the same type.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If equity value is used as numerator, use equity measure for denominator. If firm value is used as numerator, use firm measure for denominator.

methods_unique.name: Descriptional Tests
methods_unique.description: Identifying high, low, or typical values of a multiple in the market.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Understand distributional characteristics and effects of outliers on averages.

methods_unique.name: Adjusting Average PE Ratio
methods_unique.description: Adjust the average price-earnings ratio to account for firms with negative earnings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Average PE = (Sum of positive PE ratios / Number of firms with positive earnings) * Adjustment factor

methods_unique.name: Aggregating Values
methods_unique.description: Aggregate market value and net income/loss across all firms, including those with negative earnings, to compute a single price-earnings ratio.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Aggregate PE = (Total Market Value / Total Net Income)

methods_unique.name: Using Earnings Yield
methods_unique.description: Calculate the inverse of the price-earnings ratio for all firms, including those losing money.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Earnings Yield = 1 / Price-Earnings Ratio

methods_unique.name: EV-to-FCFF multiple
methods_unique.description: Calculates enterprise value based on free cash flow to firm.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV = FCF / (r - g)

methods_unique.name: PEG ratio
methods_unique.description: Adjusts the P/E ratio for expected earnings growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PEG = P/E / EPS Growth Rate

methods_unique.name: Sector Regression
methods_unique.description: Regression analysis to predict PE ratios based on risk and growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE = β0 + β1 * Risk + β2 * Growth

methods_unique.name: Market Regression
methods_unique.description: Regressing multiples against variables to predict values for individual companies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Regress any multiple (PE, EV/EBITDA, PBV) against the variables that determine each multiple using all firms in the market sample.

methods_unique.name: cluster analysis
methods_unique.description: statistical technique for finding similar firms

methods_unique.name: Price-Earnings Ratio
methods_unique.description: Ratio of market price per share to earnings per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE = Market Price Per Share / Earnings Per Share

methods_unique.name: PE Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculation of PE ratio based on different measures of earnings per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE = Price / EPS (where EPS can be trailing, current, or forward)

methods_unique.name: PE Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Uses diluted earnings per share to estimate PE ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Diluted EPS = (Net Income - Preferred Dividends) / Weighted Average Shares Outstanding (including options)

methods_unique.name: PE ratio vs. beta relationship
methods_unique.description: Analyzing how PE ratios change with varying levels of risk (beta) and growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: As beta increases, PE ratio decreases; difference in PE ratios across growth classes is less at high betas and increases as beta decreases.

methods_unique.name: Cross-sectional regression
methods_unique.description: Regression analysis of PE ratios on interest rates and expected growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE ratio = β0 + β1 * Interest Rate + β2 * Expected Growth

methods_unique.name: PE Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Comparing actual PE ratios with analyst estimates of expected growth in earnings per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Regressing the PE ratio for the sector against the expected growth rate and the emerging market dummy yields: Firms with higher growth have significantly higher PE ratios than firms with lower expected growth. Emerging market telecom firms should trade at a much lower PE ratio than those in developed markets.

methods_unique.name: Price-Earnings Ratio Regression
methods_unique.description: Regression analysis of price-earnings ratio based on growth rate, payout ratio, and beta.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE = a + bg + cπ − dβ

methods_unique.name: Regression Methodology
methods_unique.description: Compresses large amounts of data into one equation capturing the relationship between PE ratios and financial fundamentals.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE ratio = f(financial fundamentals)

methods_unique.name: PE ratio valuation
methods_unique.description: Valuation method using P/E ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected PE = regression result; Actual PE < Expected indicates undervaluation.

methods_unique.name: Normalized Earnings
methods_unique.description: Adjusting earnings for inflation and averaging over time to smooth out cyclicality.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Average EPS across a cycle, adjust for inflation.

methods_unique.name: PEG Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Uses consensus estimates of earnings per share growth over the next five years and current PEs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PEG = PE / EPS Growth Rate

methods_unique.name: PEG Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing PEG ratios in relation to firm risk and payout ratio.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: As beta increases, the PEG ratio decreases. For a given growth rate, firms with higher returns on equity should have a higher PEG ratio.

methods_unique.name: Regression Approach
methods_unique.description: Use regression analysis to control for differences in risk, growth, and payout ratios when comparing PEG ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Relate PEG ratios of firms being compared to measures of risk, growth potential, and the payout ratio.

methods_unique.name: Regression Transformation
methods_unique.description: Transform growth rate to natural log for linear relationship.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: ln(Expected Growth Rate)

methods_unique.name: Relative PE Ratio
methods_unique.description: Compare a company's PE ratio to its historical norms or to firms in different markets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE_ratio_company / PE_ratio_market

methods_unique.name: Relative PE Ratios
methods_unique.description: Adjust for market growth rate differences when comparing firm valuations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms growing slower than the market will appear cheaper at higher market growth rates.

methods_unique.name: Forward PE Ratios
methods_unique.description: Estimating future earnings growth and valuing firms with negative earnings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Calculate forward PE ratios by projecting future earnings before R&D expenses.

methods_unique.name: Forward PE
methods_unique.description: Estimate future earnings per share and divide by current stock price.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Forward PE = Current Stock Price / Expected EPS in Future Year

methods_unique.name: Forward PE Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculate forward PE using forecasted EPS.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Forward PE = Stock Price / Forecasted EPS

methods_unique.name: P/E before R&D
methods_unique.description: Adjust P/E ratio by adding back R&D expenses to net loss.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjusted EPS = Reported Net Loss + R&D Expense; Adjusted P/E = Stock Price / Adjusted EPS

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value to EBITDA Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the total market value of a firm, net of cash, relative to its EBITDA.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV/EBITDA = (Market Value of Equity + Debt - Cash) / EBITDA

methods_unique.name: EV/EBITDA calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the enterprise value to EBITDA multiple for a company.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjust numerator and denominator based on ownership percentage of subsidiaries.

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value to EBITDA Multiple Analysis
methods_unique.description: Determining the enterprise value to EBITDA multiple using a free cash flow to the firm valuation model.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV/EBITDA = (Free Cash Flow / Cost of Capital) + Terminal Value / (1 + Cost of Capital)^n - Reinvestment Requirements

methods_unique.name: Cost of Capital Method
methods_unique.description: Determines the value to EBITDA based on a firm's cost of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms with lower costs of capital should trade at higher multiples of EBITDA.

methods_unique.name: Expected Growth Method
methods_unique.description: Evaluates the impact of expected growth on the value to EBITDA.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms with higher expected growth should trade at higher multiples of EBITDA.

methods_unique.name: EV/EBITDA and Net Cap Ex Ratios
methods_unique.description: Estimates firm value multiples for stable-growth or high-growth firms based on their fundamentals.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses EV/EBITDA multiple in capital-intensive firms with heavy infrastructure investments when depreciation methods vary widely across firms.

methods_unique.name: valuation using earnings multiples
methods_unique.description: determine firm value based on expected growth, risk, and cash flow potential
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: P/E ratio = (Expected Growth / (Cost of Equity - Expected Growth)) * (1 + Tax Rate) * (1 - Reinvestment Rate)

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Divide market price per share by book value of equity per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: P/B = Market Price Per Share / Book Value Of Equity Per Share

methods_unique.name: Adjusting Book Equity for Buybacks and Acquisitions
methods_unique.description: To adjust for the differences in price-to-book ratios due to buybacks and acquisitions, take out goodwill from acquisitions and add back the market value of buybacks to book equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Book Value = Book Equity - Goodwill + Market Value of Buybacks

methods_unique.name: Price to Book Value
methods_unique.description: Measures the ratio of a company's market price to its book value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating intrinsic price-to-book ratio for firms in stable growth and high-growth scenarios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = (ROE * b) / (Ke - g), where ROE is return on equity, b is the payout ratio, Ke is cost of equity, and g is expected growth rate.

methods_unique.name: Price–Book Value Ratio
methods_unique.description: Calculates the ratio of a company's market price to its book value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price–Book Value Ratio = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_unique.name: ROE-based moat measurement
methods_unique.description: Measures company strength through ROE.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Strength of a company's moat is indicated by the level and sustainability of its Return on Equity (ROE).

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Value Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Compare PBV ratios of firms within a sector and across markets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_unique.name: Matrix Approach
methods_unique.description: Plotting price-to-book value ratios against excess returns to identify misvaluations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: None provided, but involves visual analysis of plotted data points.

methods_unique.name: Regression Analysis for PBV Ratios
methods_unique.description: Estimate price-to-book ratios using regression analysis with risk, growth, return on equity, and payout as independent variables.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = α + β1(Payout) - β2(Beta) + β3(EGR) + β4(ROE)

methods_unique.name: forecast return on equity
methods_unique.description: method to predict future performance of a firm's ROE.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1. Compute historical average ROE over past three or five years and use it as forecast if current ROE is volatile. 2. Adjust current ROE towards industry average considering competitive pressures.

methods_unique.name: PBV/ROE Quadrant Movement
methods_unique.description: A company moves from low PBV/low ROE to high PBV/high ROE as its performance improves.

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Value Ratio Strategy
methods_unique.description: Screening for undervalued stocks based on low price-to-book value ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Select stocks with a book-to-price ratio (low PBV) to achieve excess returns.

methods_unique.name: Price–Book Value Ratios
methods_unique.description: Screening stocks with low price-to-book ratios for excess returns.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Select firms with a price-to-book value ratio below a certain threshold, typically less than two-thirds of book value.

methods_unique.name: Value-to-Book Ratio
methods_unique.description: Measures firm value relative to book value of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Market Value of Debt + Market Value of Equity / Book Value of Capital

methods_unique.name: Value-to-Book Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing firm value multiples using a free cash flow to the firm valuation model.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The value-to-book ratio is fundamentally determined by its return on capital (ROC). For high-growth firms, it's also influenced by cost of capital (k_c), growth rate, and reinvestment rate.

methods_unique.name: DCF and relative valuation
methods_unique.description: Measuring returns generated by a firm on its investments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses ROC, ROIC, ROE for DCF; uses various measures for relative valuation.

methods_unique.name: D/C
methods_unique.description: Debt-to-Capital ratio calculation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Total debt/(Total debt + Market value of equity)

methods_unique.name: ROC
methods_unique.description: Return on Capital calculation.

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Value Ratio
methods_unique.description: Determines firm valuation based on its expected payout ratio, growth rate in earnings, and riskiness.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = (Expected Payout Ratio * Expected Growth Rate) / Riskiness + Return on Equity

methods_unique.name: Price-Book Value Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates PBV based on given financial metrics.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = (ROE * (1 - Dividend Payout Ratio) + Risk-Free Rate) / (Risk-Free Rate + Market Risk Premium - ROE * (1 - Dividend Payout Ratio))

methods_unique.name: Revenue Multiple
methods_unique.description: Measures equity or business value relative to revenue.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Sales Ratio (PSR) = Market Value of Equity / Revenues; EV/Sales = Enterprise Value / Revenues

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Sales Ratio
methods_unique.description: Value of equity to revenues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price-to-Sales = Equity Value / Revenues

methods_unique.name: EV-to-Sales Ratio
methods_unique.description: Multiple of the value of operating assets to revenues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV-to-Sales = Enterprise Value / Revenues

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Sales Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculation based on stable growth dividend discount model.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: P0 = EPS0(1 + g)(payout ratio) / (ke - g)

methods_unique.name: Price-Sales Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the price-sales ratio based on profit margin, payout ratio, and growth rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PS = (Sales_0 * Profit Margin) / (k_e,hg - g) + Payout_n / (k_e,st - g_n)

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Sales Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating PS ratio for high-growth firms using two-stage model.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Growth rate in first five years, cost of equity, growth rate after five years, payout ratios, net profit margin, risk-free rate are used to calculate the intrinsic price-to-sales ratio.

methods_unique.name: Two-Stage Firm Valuation Model
methods_unique.description: Uses beta, risk-free rate, and equity risk premium to estimate value-to-sales ratio.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Dividing both sides by the revenue, you get: ATOM = EBIT(1 - t)/Sales; RIR = Reinvestment rate (RIR_n is for stable growth period); k_c = Cost of capital (hg: high growth and st: stable growth periods); g = Growth rate in operating income in high growth and stable growth periods.

methods_unique.name: Two-Stage EV/Sales Equation
methods_unique.description: Estimates intrinsic enterprise value using high growth and stable growth periods.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Plugging in the growth rate, reinvestment rate, operating margin, cost of capital for both stages.

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates enterprise value using debt and cash balance.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Debt Outstanding - Cash Balance)

methods_unique.name: Revenue Multiples Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate value-to-sales ratios for stable-growth or high-growth firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value-to-Sales Ratio = (Net Margin * Sales/Book Value of Equity) + Operating Margin * Sales/Capital

methods_unique.name: Profit Margin and Turnover Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing the impact of pricing strategies on profit margins and turnover ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Divide after-tax operating income by sales to get the after-tax operating margin.

methods_unique.name: Price Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates price based on sales/book value and book value of equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price = V/S × S/BV × BV

methods_unique.name: Brand Name Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value of a brand name using profit margins and price-sales ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: V/S_b - V/S_g

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value to Sales Ratio
methods_unique.description: Estimates enterprise value based on sales.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV/Sales = Enterprise Value / Total Sales

methods_unique.name: Brand Name Valuation Method
methods_unique.description: Assesses the impact of brand name on a company's valuation by comparing it with a less branded competitor.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses financial metrics like operating margin, return on capital, and EV/Sales to estimate value loss if the brand were removed.

methods_unique.name: Revenue Multiple Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing profit margins to identify overvalued or undervalued securities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms with high revenue ratios and low profit margins are potentially overvalued, while firms with low revenue multiples and high profit margins may be undervalued.

methods_unique.name: Value/Sales and Margins Matrix
methods_unique.description: Plot firms in a sector or industry on this matrix to identify potential mismatches between margins and revenue multiples.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Compare current profit margins with expected future margins, consider the linearity of revenue multiples relative to margins, and account for risk factors.

methods_unique.name: EV-to-sales ratios analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing EV-to-sales ratios against operating margins.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms with higher operating margins tend to have higher value-to-sales ratios.

methods_unique.name: price-to-sales ratios analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing price-to-sales ratios against net margins.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Internet retailers' price-to-sales ratios plotted against their most recent year's net margins.

methods_unique.name: Cash Burn Ratio
methods_unique.description: Measures potential cash problems in firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cash Balance / |EBITDA|

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Sales Regression Analysis
methods_unique.description: Regressing price-to-sales ratio against net margins and other fundamental variables.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PS = a + b1*Payout - b2*Beta + b3*EGR + b4*Margin

methods_unique.name: Regression Analysis for EV/Sales
methods_unique.description: Uses regression analysis to predict EV/Sales ratios based on financial metrics.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EV/Sales = a + b1(Expected growth) + b2(Operating margin) - c(Tax rate) + d(Reinvestment rate)

methods_unique.name: EV-to-sales ratio valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the future value of a firm by comparing its EV-to-sales ratio to industry averages.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = (Future revenues * Industry average EV-to-sales ratio - Debt + Cash) / Shares outstanding

methods_unique.name: Regression analysis for EV/sales ratios
methods_unique.description: Use regression analysis to predict the future EV-to-sales ratio of a firm based on its expected growth rate and operating margin.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Predicted EV-to-sales = Regression equation (expected growth rate, operating margin) * Future revenues

methods_unique.name: Sector-specific multiples
methods_unique.description: Estimate value based on operating details and output.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Enterprise Value / Operating Units

methods_unique.name: Enterprise Value per Unit
methods_unique.description: Standardizes market value by dividing by units of product or service.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Enterprise Value / Units (e.g., tons of steel, subscribers)

methods_unique.name: Subscriber Valuation Model
methods_unique.description: Calculates the value of a firm based on existing and potential subscribers.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (V = NX * (VX - C) / r, where V is the total value of the firm, NX is the number of existing subscribers, VX is the net cash flow per subscriber, C is the cost to acquire a new subscriber, and r is the discount rate or cost of capital)

methods_unique.name: Subscriber Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value added by each subscriber over time.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per Subscriber = (Revenues - Costs) / Number of Subscribers

methods_unique.name: Cascading Values
methods_unique.description: Evaluating companies based on value per user/member.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Compare EV/sales multiple and value/member to assess valuation.

methods_unique.name: Price-Sales Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the appropriate price-sales multiple for a company based on its financial metrics.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PS = (Dividend Yield + Growth Rate) / (Risk-Free Rate + Market Risk Premium - Dividend Yield)

methods_unique.name: Value-to-Sales Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value-to-sales ratio based on expected growth in operating income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: V/S = (Operating Income Growth Rate / Cost of Capital) * (1 + Operating Income Growth Rate / Cost of Capital)

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow Models
methods_unique.description: Adapting DCF models to value financial service firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Consider traditional dividend discount model, cash flow to equity discount model, and excess return model.

methods_unique.name: Debt as Raw Material
methods_unique.description: Financial service firms treat debt as a raw material rather than capital.

methods_unique.name: Valuation Method for Financial Service Firms
methods_unique.description: Value equity directly rather than the entire firm due to regulatory constraints and difficulty in measuring reinvestment.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Discount expected cash flows to equity investors at the cost of equity instead of using WACC.

methods_unique.name: Bottom-up Beta Calculation
methods_unique.description: Uses average levered beta for comparable firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Beta = Average Levered Beta of Comparable Firms

methods_unique.name: Dividend Estimation from Earnings
methods_unique.description: Calculates expected dividends based on earnings and payout ratio.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected Dividends = Expected Earnings * Payout Ratio

methods_unique.name: Historical Growth Method
methods_unique.description: Estimating earnings growth based on past performance.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use historical data to predict future earnings, but be cautious if the regulatory environment is changing.

methods_unique.name: Analyst Estimates Method
methods_unique.description: Using forecasts from financial analysts for publicly traded firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Consider analyst estimates for widely followed banks and insurance companies, though their long-term accuracy may not exceed historical growth.

methods_unique.name: Fundamental Growth Model
methods_unique.description: Estimates expected EPS growth based on retention ratio and ROE.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: G = (RR * ROE)

methods_unique.name: Cost of Equity Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimates the cost of equity using beta, risk-free rate, and country risk premium.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cost of Equity = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate) + Country Risk Premium

methods_unique.name: Dividend Discount Model Adaptation
methods_unique.description: Valuing non-dividend paying financial service firms using DDM.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Expected future dividends discounted back at the cost of equity.

methods_unique.name: Capitalizing Employee Expenses
methods_unique.description: A method to capitalize and amortize employee training costs over a specified period.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Identify amortizable life, collect prior year expenses, compute current year's amortization expense, adjust net income.

methods_unique.name: Capitalization of Employee Expenses
methods_unique.description: Adjust net income by capitalizing employee development expenses.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Net Income Adjusted = Net Income + Unamortized Portion of Employee Development Expenses

methods_unique.name: Equity Capital Reinvestment
methods_unique.description: Calculating the impact of retained earnings on a firm's ability to expand its loan portfolio.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Net Income - Dividends = Retained Earnings; Retained Earnings increase equity capital, allowing for additional loans proportional to the initial leverage ratio.

methods_unique.name: Present Value of Dividends
methods_unique.description: Estimates potential dividends based on regulatory capital ratio and calculates their present value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PV = ∑(Dividend_t / (1 + r)^t) for t=1 to 5, where r is the cost of equity

methods_unique.name: Discounting Terminal Value
methods_unique.description: Calculating the value of equity by discounting future dividends and terminal value back to present.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity = Present Value of Dividends + Discounted Terminal Value

methods_unique.name: Excess Return Model
methods_unique.description: A model that values equity based on excess returns.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Equity Capital Invested + Expected Excess Returns

methods_unique.name: Expected Cash Flows Method
methods_unique.description: Valuing a loan portfolio or insurance policies based on expected cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of loans = Summation of discounted future cash flows using fair market interest rate.

methods_unique.name: Price-Earnings Ratio Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the price-earnings ratio based on expected growth rate, payout ratio, and cost of equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: P/E = (Expected Growth Rate + Payout Ratio) / Cost of Equity

methods_unique.name: Valuing a Company Based on Business Units
methods_unique.description: Breaks down net profit by business and applies PE ratio based on competitors in each segment.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cumulate equity values across businesses to determine overall company valuation.

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Ratio and Return on Equity Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing the relationship between price-to-book ratios and returns on equity for banks.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PBV = Market Price / Book Value; ROE = Net Income / Shareholder's Equity

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Model
methods_unique.description: Incorporates regulatory effects into both discount rate and expected future cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjust the discount rate for non-diversifiable regulatory risk; adjust expected growth rates based on regulatory restrictions.

methods_unique.name: Subsidy Benefit Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the value of government subsidies using expected cash flows and risk-free rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of subsidy = ∑(Expected Cash Flows * (1 + Risk-Free Rate)^-t)

methods_unique.name: Price-to-Book Ratio Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing price-to-book ratios relative to industry peers.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: P/B ratio = Market Price per Share / Book Value per Share

methods_unique.name: Loan Portfolio Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value of a loan portfolio based on interest income and market rates.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Present Value of Future Cash Flows discounted at market rate

methods_unique.name: Excess Return Approach
methods_unique.description: Valuing equity by estimating future excess returns over cost of equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Sum of Discounted Excess Returns

methods_unique.name: Earnings Growth Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating future earnings based on historical data or projections.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Growth rate = (Current Earnings - Previous Earnings) / Previous Earnings

methods_unique.name: Fundamental Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimate growth using return on equity and reinvestment rate.

methods_unique.name: Net Operating Losses Tracking
methods_unique.description: Track net operating losses to shield future income from taxes.

methods_unique.name: Temporary vs. Long-Term Problem Analysis
methods_unique.description: Determine whether negative earnings are due to temporary or long-term issues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Temporary: One-time events, economic downturns; Long-Term: Strategic missteps, operational inefficiencies, excessive debt.

methods_unique.name: Making the Call: Short-Term Versus Long-Term Problems
methods_unique.description: Guidelines for analysts to distinguish between temporary and long-term financial issues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Consider management credibility, information timeliness and detail, industry confirmation, and problem persistence.

methods_unique.name: Earnings Normalization
methods_unique.description: Adjust earnings to remove non-recurring expenses and estimate normal earnings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Remove specific event costs, including tax benefits. Normalize high expense items relative to previous years or use prior operating margins.

methods_unique.name: Adjust Expected Growth
methods_unique.description: Adjusting expected growth rate to reflect cyclical changes.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use a higher growth rate in the near term if both firm's earnings and economy are depressed but expected to recover quickly. Reverse strategy if current earnings are inflated due to an economic boom.

methods_unique.name: Valuing a Cyclical Company Using a Higher Growth Rate
methods_unique.description: Estimating future earnings and growth rates for cyclical companies during economic recovery.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Discounting projected free cash flows at the cost of capital to determine enterprise value.

methods_unique.name: Average income
methods_unique.description: Averaging operating income from a period to normalize.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Sum of operating incomes over the period / number of years

methods_unique.name: Industry average margin
methods_unique.description: Applying industry's average pretax operating margin to estimate normalized operating income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Average pretax operating margin * current year revenues

methods_unique.name: Normalized Operating Income Calculation
methods_unique.description: Averaging pretax operating margin from past years and applying it to current revenue.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Average Margin (1998-2008) = 7.33%; Normalized Operating Income = Average Margin * Current Revenue

methods_unique.name: Normalized Income Method
methods_unique.description: Evaluates stock value based on normalized income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per share = Normalized Income / Number of Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Normalized Commodity Price Valuation
methods_unique.description: Valuing firms using an average commodity price over a cycle.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Average price of the commodity over a historical period is used to estimate firm value.

methods_unique.name: Current Price with Option Value
methods_unique.description: Using current commodity prices and adding option value for future production adjustments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Current Production Value + Option Value

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow Analysis
methods_unique.description: Calculates the present value of future cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Present Value = Future Cash Flows / (1 + Cost of Capital)^n

methods_unique.name: Normalized Earnings Calculation
methods_unique.description: Adjusting earnings to reflect normal levels.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Earnings adjustments are necessary for accurate valuation, especially in cases of short-term negative impacts.

methods_unique.name: Earnings Shift Analysis
methods_unique.description: Determine if a firm's earnings shift is permanent or temporary.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If the shift is permanent, value the firm assuming it will never recover lost ground. If optimistic about recovery, assume traditional margins and growth rates.

methods_unique.name: Golden Share Valuation
methods_unique.description: A method to assess the impact of government control on a privatized firm's valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value attached to firms with golden shares will be lower due to restrictions on management changes and efficiency improvements.

methods_unique.name: DCF with Probability of Default Adjustment
methods_unique.description: Value a firm using DCF and adjust for the probability it will not survive as a going concern.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjust DCF value by (1 - probability of default)

methods_unique.name: Probability of Bankruptcy Estimation
methods_unique.description: Use statistical techniques like probits to estimate the probability of bankruptcy.

methods_unique.name: Terminal Value Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate terminal value using FCFF, cost of capital, and stable growth rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: TV = (FCFF * (1 + g)) / (WACC - g)

methods_unique.name: Probability of Default Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculate probability of default based on bond rating or bond price.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: π_distress = 1 − (1 − π_annual)^n

methods_unique.name: Default Proceeds Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate proceeds from selling assets at a percentage of book value minus liquidation costs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Proceeds = Book Value * Sale Percentage - Liquidation Costs

methods_unique.name: Distress-adjusted value
methods_unique.description: Calculates equity value considering going concern and distress/default probabilities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of equity = (Probability of Going Concern * Value in Going Concern) + (Probability of Distress/Default * Value in Default)

methods_unique.name: Valuation Method
methods_unique.description: Calculates the value of equity based on book and market values.

methods_unique.name: Valuing product options
methods_unique.description: Three approaches to valuing firms with patents.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1) Market value of product options + DCF valuation; 2) Higher growth rate in DCF analysis; 3) Option pricing model + DCF valuation.

methods_unique.name: DCF Valuation for Young Start-Ups
methods_unique.description: Valuation challenges and considerations for young start-up firms.

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow Valuation Adjustments
methods_unique.description: Adjusting DCF valuation for cyclical firms and negative earnings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjust earnings for cyclical fluctuations; consider liquidation value in distressed situations.

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF)
methods_unique.description: Estimate future cash flows and discount them to present value using cost of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: FCFF = EBIT * (1 - Tax Rate) + Depreciation - CapEx - Change in Net Working Capital

methods_unique.name: Probability-Weighted Valuation
methods_unique.description: Adjust valuation for risk of bankruptcy.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected Firm Value = P(No Bankruptcy) * Firm Value(No Bankruptcy) + P(Bankruptcy) * Firm Value(Bankruptcy)

methods_unique.name: Life Cycle Valuation
methods_unique.description: A method to assess a company's valuation based on its stage in the business life cycle.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Estimate future growth potential and value based on management competence, market presence, and comparable firms.

methods_unique.name: Venture Capital Method
methods_unique.description: Valuation method for private firms based on future earnings and multiples.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Earnings in a future year * P/E ratio of comparable public firms = Exit value; Discount exit value to present at target rate of return.

methods_unique.name: Venture Capital Target Return Calculation
methods_unique.description: Uses a high target return to account for risk and negotiate stake size.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Target Return = (Historical Experience + Judgment + Risk of Failure) * High Beta

methods_unique.name: Use of Updated Information
methods_unique.description: Utilize the most recent financial data, especially for firms with negative earnings and high revenue growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Prefer trailing 12 months' revenues and earnings over last fiscal year's figures.

methods_unique.name: Operating Margin Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating future operating margins based on current industry averages.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use the pre-tax operating margin of automotive firms globally in a given year as a target for future margins.

methods_unique.name: Imputed Return on Capital Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimate return on capital each year based on initial investment and reinvestment.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Initial capital = Balance sheet value in 2010; Subsequent years' capital = Previous year's capital + Reinvestment for the current year.

methods_unique.name: synthetic rating estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate cost of debt using expected interest coverage ratio based on future operating income forecasts.

methods_unique.name: Fully Diluted Approach
methods_unique.description: Divide the value of equity by the fully diluted number of shares.

methods_unique.name: Option Valuation Approach
methods_unique.description: Use the dilution-adjusted Black-Scholes model with market price and standard deviation to calculate option value and then determine value per share.

methods_unique.name: Value per Share and Pretax Margin
methods_unique.description: Calculates value per share based on pretax margin improvement.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The value per share increases by approximately $4/share for every 1% improvement in pretax margins.

methods_unique.name: valuation skills
methods_unique.description: Reflects the analyst's ability to value young firms with negative earnings.

methods_unique.name: Earnings Expectation Management
methods_unique.description: Managers must understand and manage investor expectations to maintain credibility.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Understand why investors value the firm as they do, communicate realistic potential without overpromising.

methods_unique.name: Probability-Weighted Expected Return
methods_unique.description: Calculate expected value considering probabilities of success and failure.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value if Success * Probability of Success + Value if Failure * Probability of Failure

methods_unique.name: Venture Capital Target Rate of Return
methods_unique.description: Determine the target rate of return for venture capitalists based on company stage.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 30% to 70%, depending on early or late startup phase

methods_unique.name: Scaling Inputs for Revenue Growth
methods_unique.description: Adjust financial inputs to reflect recent revenue growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Scale all inputs by the ratio of current year revenues to previous year revenues.

methods_unique.name: Regression Analysis for Cost of Equity
methods_unique.description: Use regression analysis to estimate cost of equity based on historical returns.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Regress stock returns against market index returns over a past period (2-5 years)

methods_unique.name: Accounting Beta
methods_unique.description: Estimate beta using changes in accounting earnings against an equity index.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Regress changes in private firm's accounting earnings against changes in earnings for an equity index (e.g., S&P 500). The slope of the regression is the accounting beta.

methods_unique.name: Fundamental Betas Regression
methods_unique.description: Relates firm betas to observable variables like ROE, debt ratios, and earnings variability.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: beta = a + b1*ROE + b2*(FA/TA) + b3*DC + b4*g

methods_unique.name: Adjusting unlevered betas
methods_unique.description: Adjusting unlevered betas for financial leverage in private firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Levered beta = Unlevered beta * (1 + (1 - tax rate) * market debt-to-equity ratio)

methods_unique.name: Total Beta Calculation
methods_unique.description: Adjusts market beta to include nondiversifiable risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Total Beta = Market Beta / ρjm, where ρjm is the correlation coefficient between the firm and the market.

methods_unique.name: Total Beta Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimates the cost of equity for a private firm by adjusting beta for leverage and tax effects.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Unlevered Beta = Levered Beta / (1 + (1 - Tax Rate) * Debt/Equity Ratio)

methods_unique.name: Build up approach
methods_unique.description: Start with expected return from a conventional risk and return model, add premiums for small cap and illiquidity risks.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected Return = Base Return + Small Cap Premium (4-5%) + Illiquidity Premium

methods_unique.name: Using recent borrowing interest rates
methods_unique.description: Use the most recent borrowing rate as a proxy for cost of debt.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cost of Debt = Recent Borrowing Interest Rate

methods_unique.name: Industry average cost of debt
methods_unique.description: Assume private firm's cost of debt will align with industry average after IPO.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cost of Debt ≈ Industry Average Cost of Debt (post-IPO)

methods_unique.name: Synthetic ratings from interest coverage ratios
methods_unique.description: Estimate synthetic bond rating based on interest coverage ratio and apply default spread.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Rating = Table 24.1 mapping; Default Spread = Rating's typical premium over riskless rate

methods_unique.name: Debt Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Use industry-average or target debt ratios for consistency in levered beta and cost of capital calculations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If industry-average debt-to-equity ratio is used to estimate the levered beta, use the same for cost of capital. If target debt-to-equity ratio is used, apply it similarly.

methods_unique.name: Tax-adjusted valuation
methods_unique.description: Adjusting cash flows for different buyer tax rates.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of a private firm can vary across buyers due to differences in marginal tax rates.

methods_unique.name: Adjusted Income Statement
methods_unique.description: Adjusting financial statements to reflect economic reality.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Add imputed depreciation and wages, convert operating lease expenses into capitalized leases.

methods_unique.name: Infinite Growth Assumption
methods_unique.description: Assumes continued growth beyond a certain period, especially in larger private firms with professional management.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Growth Rate > 0% after initial projection period

methods_unique.name: Illiquidity Discount Factors
methods_unique.description: Identifies factors affecting illiquidity discount.

methods_unique.name: Illiquidity Discount Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the illiquidity discount based on empirical studies and regression analysis.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: RPRS = Restricted stock price/Unrestricted stock price = 1 − Illiquidity discount

methods_unique.name: Bid-Ask Spread Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimating illiquidity discounts by comparing bid-ask spreads of publicly traded stocks to variables measurable in private businesses.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Regress the bid-ask spread against firm revenues and profitability status, then extend regression to a larger sample.

methods_unique.name: Restricted Stock Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimates the illiquidity discount based on revenue and a base discount.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Base discount for $10 million in revenues is 25%, additional discount of 3.75% for smaller revenues, resulting in an estimated discount of 28.75%. (Silber equation)

methods_unique.name: illiquidity discount estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimates illiquidity discount using restricted stock approach and bid-ask spread approach.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Based on earnings positivity and cash-to-revenue ratio, estimate illiquidity discount.

methods_unique.name: Valuation for Sale to a Private Entity
methods_unique.description: Uses total beta and private business tax rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cost of equity based on total beta, cost of debt may reflect additional spread.

methods_unique.name: Valuation for Publicly Traded Firm or IPO
methods_unique.description: Uses market beta and corporate marginal tax rates.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cost of equity based on market beta, cost of debt based on synthetic rating.

methods_unique.name: Valuation Method with Control Premium
methods_unique.description: Subtracting equity options' value from total equity and dividing by shares outstanding.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of Equity - Value of Options / Shares Outstanding

methods_unique.name: Control Issue Valuation
methods_unique.description: Calculating the difference in firm valuation under optimal management versus current management to determine control premium.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Value of Firm Under Optimal Management - Value of Firm Under Incumbent Management) * Proportion of Controlling Interest

methods_unique.name: Valuing Venture Capital and Private Equity Stakes
methods_unique.description: Adjusts valuation approach based on investor diversification level.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Total Beta = Portfolio Correlation with Market

methods_unique.name: Beta Adjustment for Private Companies
methods_unique.description: Adjust beta based on the correlation of the company's business with the market and the investor's portfolio diversification.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Perceived beta = 1 / Correlation; Cost of equity = Risk-free rate + Perceived beta * Equity risk premium

methods_unique.name: Cash Inflow Valuation
methods_unique.description: Determines firm value based on whether cash is added to or removed from the firm.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = Operating Assets + Cash (if not taken out by existing owners)

methods_unique.name: Private Company Valuation via Transaction Multiples
methods_unique.description: Uses historical transaction data of similar private companies to estimate the value of a target company.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = (Transaction Value / Scaling Variable) * Target's Scaling Variable

methods_unique.name: Scale to variables less affected by discretionary choices
methods_unique.description: Focus on revenue multiples over earnings multiples due to lower manipulation risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use revenue-based multiples instead of earnings-based multiples.

methods_unique.name: Value businesses, not equity
methods_unique.description: Classify and use enterprise value multiples rather than equity multiples for private companies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Enterprise Value = Market Value of Equity + Debt - Cash and Equivalents

methods_unique.name: Start with a large dataset
methods_unique.description: Collect transaction data from a wide range of companies to screen out suspicious transactions.

methods_unique.name: Adjust for timing differences
methods_unique.description: Make adjustments to the value of transactions to account for timing differences.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Reduce transaction prices from earlier periods by the market drop during that period.

methods_unique.name: Adjusting for life cycle differences
methods_unique.description: Account for the fundamental differences between public and private firms due to their lifecycle stage.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Consider growth potential, market establishment level when comparing public multiples with private firm valuation.

methods_unique.name: Liquidity discount
methods_unique.description: Factor in the liquidity difference between public and private equities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjust valuation downwards when applying public multiples to illiquid private business assets.

methods_unique.name: Adjust for survival
methods_unique.description: Account for higher risk of failure in private businesses.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use public company multiples but adjust downward to reflect the increased risk.

methods_unique.name: Adjust for nondiversification
methods_unique.description: Apply a higher cost of equity due to undiversified risks.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If average PE ratio for similar public companies is 12, apply a lower PE ratio for private firms with same characteristics.

methods_unique.name: Adjust for illiquidity
methods_unique.description: Estimate and incorporate illiquidity discounts in valuation multiples.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use techniques from intrinsic valuation to adjust public multiples for illiquidity.

methods_unique.name: Adjusting public multiple for illiquidity
methods_unique.description: Techniques to adjust the public multiple value for illiquidity.

methods_unique.name: Silber regression
methods_unique.description: A method to determine liquidity discounts for private sales.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses historical data on transaction prices and market values of similar firms.

methods_unique.name: bid-ask spread equation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the illiquidity discount.

methods_unique.name: Acquisition Premium Calculation
methods_unique.description: Determines the difference between acquisition price and market price prior to acquisition.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Acquisition Premium = Acquisition Price - Market Price Prior to Acquisition

methods_unique.name: Acquisition Price Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing the price paid for an acquisition compared to initial offer and book value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Compare acquisition price with initial offer and adjusted book value of equity.

methods_unique.name: Takeover Premium Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing the premiums paid in tender offers and mergers.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Premiums averaged 19% in the 1960s, 35% in the 1970s, and 30% between 1980-1985.

methods_unique.name: Impact of Takeover Failures
methods_unique.description: Evaluating stock price reactions to takeover failures.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Substantial number of target firms are taken over within 60 days post-failure, earning significant excess returns (50%-66%).

methods_unique.name: Acquire Undervalued Firms
methods_unique.description: Identify and acquire companies trading below their intrinsic value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Requires superior information or analytical tools, access to capital, and a proven track record of successful acquisitions.

methods_unique.name: synergy analysis
methods_unique.description: Identifying potential value from combining two firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Evaluate economies of scale, pricing power, functional strengths combination.

methods_unique.name: Financial Synergy
methods_unique.description: Combination of firms with excess cash and high-return projects.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value increase from projects funded by excess cash.

methods_unique.name: Debt Capacity Increase
methods_unique.description: Combined earnings stability allows for higher borrowing limits.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Tax benefit from increased debt capacity, shown as higher cash flows or lower cost of capital.

methods_unique.name: Synergy Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates whether a merger creates additional value beyond the sum of its parts.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: V(AB) > V(A) + V(B)

methods_unique.name: Intrinsic Value Framework
methods_unique.description: Actions that enhance value increase cash flows from existing assets, expected growth rates, length of the growth period, or reduce cost of capital.

methods_unique.name: Ego Discount
methods_unique.description: Adjusting firm valuation based on managerial self-interest.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If a strong-willed CEO is replaced, the future return on capital may improve.

methods_unique.name: Identifying Undervalued Targets
methods_unique.description: Use relative valuation or discounted cash flow approaches to identify undervalued firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Relative valuation: compare multiples (P/E, P/BV, etc.) against industry peers. DCF valuation: compare market price to estimated intrinsic value.

methods_unique.name: Diversification Target Selection
methods_unique.description: Select targets in businesses unrelated and uncorrelated with the acquiring firm's business.

methods_unique.name: Status Quo Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the firm value with existing policies before considering control and synergy premiums.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of the firm = function of cash flows, growth in cash flows, high-growth period length, cost of capital

methods_unique.name: Free Cash Flow Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimates firm value based on future free cash flows discounted at the cost of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firm Value = ∑(FCFF_t / (1 + WACC)^t) for t=1 to 5 + Terminal Value / (1 + WACC)^5

methods_unique.name: Value of Control
methods_unique.description: Calculates the additional value a firm could achieve through restructuring.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The value of control is significant for firms operating below optimal levels, as it can lead to substantial increases in value through improved management and financial structure.

methods_unique.name: Synergy Valuation Method
methods_unique.description: Answering two fundamental questions to estimate the form and impact of synergy on value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1. What form is the synergy expected to take? Will it reduce costs or increase growth potential? 2. Does it influence cash flows, growth rates, growth period, or cost of capital?

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Techniques
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of synergies in mergers and acquisitions.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value the combined firm with synergy built in, then subtract the pre-acquisition values to find synergy value.

methods_unique.name: Merger Synergy Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value added by synergies in a merger.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of synergy = Value of combined firm (with synergy) - Value of combined firm (without synergy)

methods_unique.name: Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC)
methods_unique.description: Calculates the cost of capital for a firm based on its debt and equity weights.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: WACC = (E/V) * Re + (D/V) * Rd * (1 - Tc)

methods_unique.name: Cash Slack
methods_unique.description: Firms with excess cash may acquire firms with capital constraints to finance profitable projects.

methods_unique.name: Tax Benefits
methods_unique.description: Combining firms can result in tax benefits from unused tax deductions and asset write-ups leading to higher depreciation tax savings.

methods_unique.name: Debt Capacity Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimating firm value after a merger based on increased debt capacity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of the combined firm = Current Value + (Increase in Debt Capacity * Beta Adjustment Factor)

methods_unique.name: Acquisition Valuation
methods_unique.description: Evaluating the financial impact of acquisitions on stock prices and earnings per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If the price paid for an acquisition exceeds its fair market value, the acquiring firm's stock price will decline. An acquisition is considered accretive if it increases EPS but this does not necessarily reflect true value.

methods_unique.name: Comparable Firm Selection
methods_unique.description: Choosing firms for comparison in valuation.

methods_unique.name: Multiple Selection
methods_unique.description: Selecting a multiple such as P/E, P/B, or P/S ratios.

methods_unique.name: Matching Discount Rates with Cash Flows
methods_unique.description: Ensuring that the discount rate used reflects the riskiness of the cash flows being discounted.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use the target firm's cost of equity to discount its pretax cash flows, not the acquirer's cost of equity.

methods_unique.name: Cost of Capital Discounting
methods_unique.description: Uses cost of capital to discount cash flows to equity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Always wrong and results in overvaluation.

methods_unique.name: Multiple Bidders Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyzing stock returns around takeover announcements.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Market-adjusted stock returns for successful bidders in single-bidder takeovers are estimated to be 2%, while in contested takeovers, they are -1.33%.

methods_unique.name: Setting the Exchange Ratio
methods_unique.description: Determine a fair exchange ratio based on intrinsic values of firms' shares to avoid mispricing.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Exchange ratio = (Intrinsic value per share of target firm) / (Intrinsic value per share of acquiring firm)

methods_unique.name: Exchange Ratio Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimates exchange ratio for acquisitions based on value of control and synergy.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses exchratio.xls spreadsheet

methods_unique.name: Goodwill Measurement
methods_unique.description: Assesses the impact of goodwill on deal valuation and cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Goodwill should not alter the fundamental value of a target company unless it qualifies for tax deductions.

methods_unique.name: Restructuring Charges Analysis
methods_unique.description: Categorizes restructuring charges into three groups based on their impact on earnings and cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1. Cash expenses reduce expected cash flows; 2. Noncash, tax-deductible expenses increase cash flows by reducing taxes; 3. Noncash, non-tax-deductible expenses affect earnings but not cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Mergers Evaluation
methods_unique.description: Evaluating mergers based on size difference and synergy type.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms of equal size have lower success probability than acquisitions by larger firms; cost-saving mergers perform better.

methods_unique.name: Going Private Valuation
methods_unique.description: Incorporating long-term value maximization into cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Increased efficiency from going private can be reflected in higher operating margins and return on capital, but limited access to financial markets may reduce future growth.

methods_unique.name: Valuing a Leveraged Buyout
methods_unique.description: Estimate free cash flow to the firm and discount it back at a cost of capital that varies from year to year.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Discount free cash flows using period-specific cost of capital.

methods_unique.name: Cash Flow Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating cash flows to the firm by subtracting net capital expenditures and changes in working capital from after-tax operating income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (After-Tax Operating Income) - (Net Capital Expenditures + Changes in Working Capital)

methods_unique.name: Acquisition Value Analysis
methods_unique.description: Evaluates acquisitions based on reasons and target firm characteristics.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value created = (Present value of future cash flows - Cost of acquisition)

methods_unique.name: Merger Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimate value of firms before and after merger, considering growth rates, cost structures, and synergies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis to estimate firm values under different scenarios.

methods_unique.name: Leveraged Buyout (LBO) Analysis
methods_unique.description: Project future cash flows for Boston Turkey to estimate equity value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cash Flow to Equity = Cash Flow to Firm - Interest Expense + Depreciation - Capital Expenditures

methods_unique.name: Discounting with cumulative cost
methods_unique.description: Adjusts discount rate for changes in cost of capital over time.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cash flow in year n is discounted using a compounded cost of capital from the present to year n.

methods_unique.name: Price Index Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating price indices for classes of real estate assets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not specified in the text.

methods_unique.name: Illiquidity Discount Method
methods_unique.description: Valuing illiquid assets as if they were liquid and then applying an illiquidity discount.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Discount rate = Liquid asset discount rate - Illiquidity premium

methods_unique.name: Survey Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimating discount rates for real estate investments by surveying potential investors.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not based on abstract models but on actual investor preferences and desired returns.

methods_unique.name: Risk and Return Model
methods_unique.description: Measures risk and estimates discount rates based on risk measures.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected return = f(risk measure)

methods_unique.name: Cost of Capital vs Cost of Equity
methods_unique.description: Determine discount rate based on whether cash flows are pre-debt or post-debt.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use cost of capital for pre-debt cash flows, cost of equity for post-debt cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Vacancy Rate Projection
methods_unique.description: Estimate the percentage of space that will not be rented out.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Vacancy rate = (Unrented space / Total space) * 100%

methods_unique.name: Discounted Cash Flow Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculate the value of a building based on its future cash flows, considering occupancy time and lease terms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: DCF Value = ∑(Cash Flows_t / (1 + r)^t) for t=0 to n

methods_unique.name: Lease Term Analysis
methods_unique.description: Analyze the impact of lease length and provisions on cash flow estimates.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Net Lease: Tenant pays taxes, insurance, maintenance

methods_unique.name: Inflation-based Terminal Value
methods_unique.description: Estimate terminal value by increasing current asset value at expected inflation rate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: terminal value = current value × (1 + inflation rate)^n

methods_unique.name: Capitalization Rate Method
methods_unique.description: Use cap rate, derived from surveys or DCF models, to value properties based on operating income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: cap rate = r - g

methods_unique.name: DCF Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value of an income-producing real asset by discounting cash flows to the firm or equity investors at respective costs of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(Cash Flow_t / (1 + r)^t), where t is time period and r is cost of capital

methods_unique.name: Gross Income Multiplier
methods_unique.description: Standardizes asset value using income.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Price of property / Gross annual income

methods_unique.name: valuation methods for real estate firms
methods_unique.description: approach valuation based on income generation type
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Assess fees as a percent of gross income and project fee growth; cost efficiency and reputation impact value

methods_unique.name: Valuation Method for Real Estate Businesses
methods_unique.description: Value each property and aggregate them, considering the business's ability to buy undervalued properties.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not specified in text

methods_unique.name: valuation assumptions
methods_unique.description: Assume high dividend payout for REITs and MLPs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Earnings will largely be paid out in dividends, limiting per-share earnings growth without external financing.

methods_unique.name: growth limitations
methods_unique.description: Estimate low per share earnings growth due to restrictions on activities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected growth in net income can be high with external financing but must be offset by proportional increase in shares.

methods_unique.name: Price per square foot valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of a building based on comparable sales data.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Total Price / Total Square Footage

methods_unique.name: Gross Rent Multiplier (GRM) Valuation
methods_unique.description: Determine property value by comparing it to similar properties using GRM.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Property Value = Gross Annual Income / GRM

methods_unique.name: Valuation of Franchises
methods_unique.description: Evaluating the value of a franchise based on its ability to generate excess returns.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Present Value of Excess Returns

methods_unique.name: Valuing a New York City Cab Medallion
methods_unique.description: Illustrates how to estimate the value of a cab medallion by calculating cash flows and discount rates.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Cash Flows = Revenues - Operating Expenses; Discount Rate = Risk-Free Rate + Beta * (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_unique.name: Valuation Method for Key Person Effect
methods_unique.description: Estimate the impact of losing a key person on cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Value with key person - Value without key person

methods_unique.name: Growing Annuity Equation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the present value of future cash flows with a constant growth rate.

methods_unique.name: Valuing Key Person
methods_unique.description: Estimate impact on occupancy and cash flows if key person is replaced.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value decline = Difference in value with and without key person.

methods_unique.name: Comparable Assets Pricing
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value of an asset by comparing it to similar assets.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = f(Price, Condition, Rarity)

methods_unique.name: Intrinsic Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimating value based on fundamentals like cash flows, growth, and risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = (Cash Flows / Risk) * Growth

methods_unique.name: Option to Delay
methods_unique.description: Evaluating projects based on their potential future value rather than current NPV.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Consider the volatility of cash flows and discount rates over time, especially in exclusive rights scenarios.

methods_unique.name: Variance Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate variance in cash flows from similar past projects, assign probabilities to market scenarios, or use the average variance of firms in the same business.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: None provided, but involves probabilistic and simulation methods.

methods_unique.name: Cost of Delay Calculation
methods_unique.description: Estimating the cost associated with delaying investment in a project.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Each year of delay translates into one less year of value-creating cash flows. If cash flows are evenly distributed over time, and the life of the patent is n years, the cost of delay can be written as: (n - t) * annual_cash_flow

methods_unique.name: Adjusting Discount Rate
methods_unique.description: Use a higher discount rate to lower the value of underlying asset.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Present Value = Future Cash Flows / (1 + Discount Rate)^n

methods_unique.name: Illiquidity Discount
methods_unique.description: Apply an illiquidity discount to the option value due to difficulty in trading.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Adjusted Option Value = Original Option Value - Illiquidity Discount

methods_unique.name: Binomial Tree
methods_unique.description: A model used for option valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Estimate the value of an option through a binomial tree, which represents possible asset price paths over time.

methods_unique.name: Patent Valuation using Option Pricing Model
methods_unique.description: Valuing a patent as an option based on the present value of costs and expected cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If I is the present value of development costs, V is the present value of expected cash flows, then the patent can be valued using the Black-Scholes model adjusted for dividends.

methods_unique.name: Valuing Call Option
methods_unique.description: Calculating the value of a call option with changing patent life.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The decline in present value of cash flows (S) and increase in cost of delay (y) reduce expected value of the patent.

methods_unique.name: Patent Option Valuation
