methods_unique.description: Estimates the value of a patent as an option considering competitive pressures.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = max(Patent Value - Discounted Cash Flow, 0)

methods_unique.name: Valuing a Firm with Patents
methods_unique.description: Estimating firm value based on three components: existing product cash flows, uncommercialized patent values, and expected future patents.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = (Cash Flows from Existing Products) + (Option Pricing Model Value of Uncommercialized Patents) + (Expected Future Patent Value)

methods_unique.name: Natural Resource Option Valuation
methods_unique.description: Valuing undeveloped reserves as call options.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Inputs: Available reserves, estimated value if extracted today. Payoff function resembles a call option.

methods_unique.name: Option Pricing Model for Natural Resource Reserves
methods_unique.description: Calculates the value of natural resource reserves using option pricing principles.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = (Reserve Quantity * Contribution per Unit) - Development Cost

methods_unique.name: Rainbow Option Pricing
methods_unique.description: A method to value options with multiple sources of variance.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Keep variances separate and account for different evolution over time.

methods_unique.name: Option Valuation for Natural Resource Reserves
methods_unique.description: A method to value undeveloped reserves as a single option, considering the underlying asset's value, exercise price, life of the option, variance in asset value, and dividend yield.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Inputs include: Value of underlying asset (cumulated undeveloped reserves), Exercise price (cost to develop all reserves), Life of the option (weighted average of reserve lives), Variance in asset value (oil price volatility), Dividend yield (cash flow from developing reserves).

methods_unique.name: NPV calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the net present value of a project.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: NPV = ∑ [Cash Flow_t / (1 + r)^t] - Initial Investment

methods_unique.name: Option to Expand
methods_unique.description: Evaluating expansion potential based on present value of cash flows and cost of expansion.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PV of future cash flows > cost of expansion

methods_unique.name: Monte Carlo simulations
methods_unique.description: Simulating project outcomes using probability distributions.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Estimate standard deviation in the value of the project over time, not at a point in time.

methods_unique.name: R&D as a call option
methods_unique.description: Treat R&D expenditure as an investment with uncertain future payoffs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The cost of the call option is the amount spent on R&D, and the payoff is contingent upon the viability of resulting products or projects.

methods_unique.name: Multistage Investment Method
methods_unique.description: Investing in stages to gauge market demand and reduce risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Evaluate projects in stages, considering the value of expansion options.

methods_unique.name: Sequential and Compound Options
methods_unique.description: An option on an option or a sequence of options where the value of each option is dependent on previous outcomes.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not provided in text, but involves modifying Black-Scholes or binomial models for accurate valuation.

methods_unique.name: Option Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Determines the value of an expansion option based on competitive advantages.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of Option = f(Competitive Advantage, Potential for Excess Returns)

methods_unique.name: Estimating Period of Competitive Advantage
methods_unique.description: Calculate excess returns over the period of competitive advantage.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Only consider excess returns earned during this period for option valuation.

methods_unique.name: Valuing Expansion Option
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of an expansion option by considering market entry costs, expected cash flows, and exclusivity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Discounted Cash Flow Value + Value Associated with Option to Expand

methods_unique.name: Valuing Financial Flexibility
methods_unique.description: Assessing the value of maintaining excess debt capacity or large cash balances as an option for future projects.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: S, K, σs, t are used to estimate annual reinvestment needs and flexibility in raising funds beyond internal sources.

methods_unique.name: Financial Flexibility Estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimating reinvestments and internal funds as a percentage of firm value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Reinvestment needs - Internal fund generation = Equity issuance. Optimal debt ratio calculation using cost of capital framework.

methods_unique.name: Financial Flexibility Option
methods_unique.description: Estimating the value of financial flexibility as an option.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Using a spreadsheet like finflex.xls to estimate the value of financial flexibility based on project returns, earnings stability, and access to external capital markets.

methods_unique.name: Option Pricing Approach
methods_unique.description: Estimates and incorporates the value of abandonment in investment projects.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: V is the remaining value if project continues, L is liquidation value; if V > L continue, else abandon. This resembles a put option.

methods_unique.name: Monte Carlo simulation
methods_unique.description: Estimates project value uncertainty.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Standard deviation of 25%

methods_unique.name: Abandonment Option Valuation
methods_unique.description: Estimate the value of an option to abandon an investment.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use a spreadsheet like 'abandon.xls' to estimate abandonment value.

methods_unique.name: Decision Trees
methods_unique.description: A method for estimating project value by considering probabilities of different outcomes and their cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = ∑(P_i * PV(CF_i)) where P_i is the probability of outcome i and CF_i are the cash flows from that outcome.

methods_unique.name: Binomial Tree Approach
methods_unique.description: A method for valuing real options by considering two possible outcomes at each node.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = max(Exercise Value, Continuation Value) discounted back to present value using replicating portfolio or adjusted discount rate.

methods_unique.name: Expansion Option Valuation
methods_unique.description: Evaluates the value added by potential future expansion opportunities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of Expansion = NPV + Premium for Potential to Expand

methods_unique.name: Abandonment Option Analysis
methods_unique.description: Assesses the benefit of having an option to abandon a project or investment.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Reduces exposure to worst-case scenarios, impacting decision to invest

methods_unique.name: Net Present Value (NPV) with Expansion Options
methods_unique.description: Calculate NPV considering potential expansion options that can offset negative initial investment NPV.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Assess conditions under which expansion has option value, then use standard deviation and risk-free rate to estimate present value of future cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Equity as a Call Option
methods_unique.description: View equity as a call option where the firm's assets are the underlying asset and debt face value is the exercise price.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use Black-Scholes model to value equity as a call option with given inputs: firm asset value, standard deviation of asset value, face value of debt, time to maturity, risk-free rate.

methods_unique.name: Equity Value and Volatility
methods_unique.description: Value of equity is a function of firm value volatility.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: As standard deviation in firm value increases, holding other factors constant, the value of equity also increases.

methods_unique.name: Multiple of Revenues Method
methods_unique.description: Estimate firm value by applying a multiple of revenues from healthy mature firms in the same business.

methods_unique.name: duration-weighted average
methods_unique.description: Calculates the duration of each debt issue and calculates a face-value-weighted average of the durations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Duration = (Σ(face value * duration) / Σface value)

methods_unique.name: Face Value of Debt Calculation
methods_unique.description: Determines how to calculate the face value of debt for distressed firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: 1. Sum principal due on all debt; 2. Include expected interest and coupon payments; 3. Consider only principal with interest as dividend yield in option pricing model.

methods_unique.name: Option Pricing for Equity
methods_unique.description: Estimate equity value in troubled firms as an option.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use spreadsheet tool to estimate the value of deep out-of-the-money options.

methods_unique.name: Deep Out-of-the-Money Options Strategy
methods_unique.description: Investing in deep out-of-the-money options with expectation of high returns from a few successful investments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Allocate to deeply troubled firms in volatile sectors; longer-term debt enhances option value.

methods_unique.name: Convertible Debt Investment
methods_unique.description: Involves taking an active role and obtaining equity stake by making the debt convertible when investing in financially troubled firms' debt.

methods_unique.name: default spread estimation
methods_unique.description: Estimate default spread over risk-free rate based on firm's asset value, debt face value, and duration.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use d1 and d2 values derived from given inputs to estimate the default spread.

methods_unique.name: Value Creation through Cash Flow Management
methods_unique.description: Identifying and managing existing assets to optimize cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Evaluate investments against cost of capital, consider continuing value vs liquidation/salvage value.

methods_unique.name: Divestiture Value Analysis
methods_unique.description: Determines the optimal action for an existing project based on continuing value, liquidation value, and divestiture value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If divestiture is optimal: Divestiture value > Present value of expected cash flows from divested assets.

methods_unique.name: Divestiture Value Calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates the net effect of divesting a division on the firm's value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Net Effect = Divestiture Value - Cost of Capital * Present Value of Future Cash Flows

methods_unique.name: Margin Analysis
methods_unique.description: Evaluates the impact of changes in operating margins on value per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value per Share = f(Operating Margin)

methods_unique.name: Cost Cutting Phasing
methods_unique.description: Evaluate the credibility of management's cost cutting claims and allow for phasing in over time.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Time to implement cost cuts = function(size_of_firm, magnitude_of_cost_cuts)

methods_unique.name: Tax Rate Optimization
methods_unique.description: Adjusting the effective tax rate while maintaining marginal tax rate to maximize after-tax cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value of firm increases more when reducing effective tax rate than changing marginal tax rate.

methods_unique.name: Net Capital Expenditure Management
methods_unique.description: Optimizing net capital expenditures by balancing maintenance and growth investments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Firms can increase value by reducing unnecessary capital expenditures on existing assets, potentially creating cash inflows.

methods_unique.name: Reducing noncash working capital
methods_unique.description: Decreasing noncash working capital as a percentage of revenues to increase cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Noncash WC reduction = (Initial Noncash WC % - Target Noncash WC %) * Revenues

methods_unique.name: Noncash WC and Value
methods_unique.description: Analyzes effect on firm value from changes in noncash working capital as a percent of revenues.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: FCFF = EBIT(1 - Tax rate) (1 - Reinvestment rate), Expected growth = Reinvestment rate × Return on capital

methods_unique.name: Reinvestment Rate and Expected Growth
methods_unique.description: Calculates expected growth based on reinvestment rate and return on capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected growth = Reinvestment rate × Return on capital

methods_unique.name: Reinvestment Rate Impact
methods_unique.description: Analyzing how changes in reinvestment rates affect the value per share.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If a firm's return on capital exceeds its cost of capital, increasing the reinvestment rate positively impacts equity value. Conversely, if the return on capital is below the cost of capital, an increase in reinvestment rate negatively affects equity value.

methods_unique.name: Growth and Margin Trade-off
methods_unique.description: Analyzing the trade-off between revenue growth and operating margins.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Revenue Growth + Operating Margins = Total Value Creation

methods_unique.name: Sales-to-Capital Ratio Impact
methods_unique.description: Calculating the effect of sales-to-capital ratio on reinvestment needs and cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Higher Sales-to-Capital Ratio -> Lower Reinvestment Needs -> Higher Cash Flows

methods_unique.name: Increasing Value Through Competitive Advantages
methods_unique.description: Firms can increase value by extending high-growth periods with excess returns through barriers to entry and nurturing competitive advantages.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = f(Barriers_to_Entry, Excess_Returns_Period)

methods_unique.name: Switching Costs
methods_unique.description: A strategy that makes it difficult for customers to switch from a company's products to those of competitors.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: None provided, but involves making conversion processes seamless while increasing barriers for switching to competitors.

methods_unique.name: Economies of Scale
methods_unique.description: Larger firms can reduce costs per unit through increased production volume.

methods_unique.name: Exclusive Distribution Rights
methods_unique.description: Firms with exclusive rights to a distribution system have an advantage over competitors in attracting customers.

methods_unique.name: Reducing Operating Risk
methods_unique.description: Making products and services less discretionary to customers.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Lowering unlevered beta (cost of equity) and cost of debt default risk.

methods_unique.name: Reduce Operating Leverage
methods_unique.description: Decreasing the proportion of fixed costs in a firm's cost structure.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Tying expenses to revenues, using outside contractors for services, leading to lower unlevered beta, reduced cost of debt and increased optimal debt ratio.

methods_unique.name: Change Financing Mix
methods_unique.description: Adjusting the mix of debt and equity financing.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Debt is cheaper than equity due to tax advantages but increases risk; firm value maximization must consider impact on operations and cash flows.

methods_unique.name: firm value maximization
methods_unique.description: replaces cost of capital minimization as objective when financing mix is based on maximizing firm value.

methods_unique.name: Debt Matching
methods_unique.description: Matching cash flows on debt to cash flows on the asset.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Reduce risk of default, increase capacity to carry debt, reduce cost of capital, and increase firm value.

methods_unique.name: Miller-Modigliani Theorem Application
methods_unique.description: Understanding how real-world factors like taxes and default risk affect the theorem's assumptions.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: In a world with taxes and default risk, changing financing mix can affect firm value due to tax advantages and bankruptcy costs.

methods_unique.name: Value Trade-off Analysis
methods_unique.description: Determine if an action is a pure value creator or involves trade-offs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Evaluate actions based on their impact on cash flows, cost of capital, and growth. Determine net effect on firm's value.

methods_unique.name: Value Enhancement Chain
methods_unique.description: Categorizes actions based on their impact and control over outcome.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Quick Fixes: Immediate value creation with firm control; Odds On: Near/medium term value creation with significant firm control; Long Term: Strategic initiatives for long-term value.

methods_unique.name: Cost of Capital Analysis
methods_unique.description: Calculates equity value based on cost of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Equity Value = Present Value of Future Cash Flows discounted at Cost of Capital

methods_unique.name: diagnose specific problems
methods_unique.description: Identify unique issues faced by a firm.

methods_unique.name: Economic Value Added (EVA)
methods_unique.description: Measures a firm's economic profit by subtracting the cost of capital from after-tax operating profits.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EVA = NOPAT - (WACC * Capital Invested)

methods_unique.name: Cash Flow Return on Investment (CFROI)
methods_unique.description: measures the percentage return made by a firm on its existing investments

methods_unique.name: Economic Value Added (EVA) Approach
methods_unique.description: Calculates firm value by summing the capital invested in assets, EVA from existing assets, and PV of future investments' EVA.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value = Capital Invested + EVA from Assets + PV(EVA from Future Investments)

methods_unique.name: EVA valuation
methods_unique.description: Calculates present value of EVA over a period and terminal year.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PV(EVA) = ∑(EVA_t / (1 + WACC)^t) for t=1 to 10, PV(Terminal EVA) based on imputed capital invested.

methods_unique.name: Economic Value Added (EVA) Compensation
methods_unique.description: Compensation based on current year's EVA with a portion held back for future years.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: EVA = NOPAT - (WACC * Invested Capital)

methods_unique.name: Economic Value Added (EVA) calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates EVA based on a formula involving cost of capital and return.

methods_unique.name: CFROI
methods_unique.description: Calculates the internal rate of return on existing investments based on real cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: CFROI = IRR(GCF, GI, n, SV)

methods_unique.name: CFROI calculation
methods_unique.description: Calculates return on investment using gross investment and salvage value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Gross Cash Flow - Salvage Value) / (Gross Investment - Salvage Value)

methods_unique.name: CFROI approach
methods_unique.description: Modifies cash flow return on investment to account for growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses both past and future cash flows, unlike IRR which is based solely on expected future cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Present Value of Excess Cash Flows
methods_unique.description: Calculates the present value based on future investments' CFROI and real cost of capital.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PV = Σ (CFROI_t,NI - k_c) / (1 + k_c)^t

methods_unique.name: Reduce gross investment
methods_unique.description: Decrease investment in existing assets to boost CFROI.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: CFROI = (Cash Inflows - Cash Outflows) / Gross Investment

methods_unique.name: Sacrifice future growth
methods_unique.description: Prioritize current asset performance over potential future growth opportunities.

methods_unique.name: Return on Capital
methods_unique.description: Estimate return on capital before and after adjusting for operating leases.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use market value of equity when available.

methods_unique.name: Net Present Value (NPV) assumption
methods_unique.description: Assumption that depreciation is offset by capital maintenance expenditures for ease of NPV calculation.

methods_unique.name: Scenario Analysis
methods_unique.description: Estimates expected cash flows and asset values under various scenarios to better understand risk's effect on value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not provided in the text, but involves setting each input into asset value to its best or worst possible outcome.

methods_unique.name: Best-Case/Worst-Case Analysis
methods_unique.description: Setting each input into asset value to its best (or worst) possible outcome and estimating cash flows with those values.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not applicable

methods_unique.name: Feasible Best-Case Scenario
methods_unique.description: Defining the best-case scenario in terms of what is feasible while allowing for the relationship between inputs.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Not applicable

methods_unique.name: Decision Tree Analysis
methods_unique.description: A method for making decisions under uncertainty by mapping out possible scenarios and their probabilities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Expected Value = Σ (Probability of Outcome * Value of Outcome)

methods_unique.name: Simulation
methods_unique.description: Examines consequences of continuous risk by generating distributions for each parameter in valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Draw one outcome from each distribution to generate unique set of cash flows and value. Repeat across large number of simulations.

methods_unique.name: Cross-sectional data substitution
methods_unique.description: Use differences in a specific variable across existing similar investments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Substitute the distribution of pretax operating margins from software companies or find a close standardized statistical distribution.

methods_unique.name: Statistical Distribution Selection
methods_unique.description: Selecting a statistical distribution that best captures variability in input data.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Choose distributions (e.g., uniform, normal) based on characteristics of the variable. For software company margins: Uniform(0%, 35%). For revenue growth: Normal(15%, 10%).

methods_unique.name: Check for correlation across variables
methods_unique.description: Identify correlations between inputs like interest rates and inflation before running simulations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: If strong correlation exists, either focus on the more impactful input or use advanced simulation packages to incorporate correlation.

methods_unique.name: Margin of Safety
methods_unique.description: A measure used by value investors to control risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Requires a stock be priced more than X percent below the estimated value before purchase.

methods_unique.name: Simulation with Constraints
methods_unique.description: Used when there is a constraint that, if violated, creates very large costs for the firm.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Examines likelihood of constraint violation and implications for value.

methods_unique.name: Regulatory Capital Restrictions
methods_unique.description: Financial service firms must maintain book equity above regulatory ratios.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Maintain book value of equity as a fraction of loans or other assets at or above a floor ratio specified by the authorities.

methods_unique.name: simulations
methods_unique.description: Assess probability and consequences of negative book value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use simulations to capture the likelihood and costs associated with a firm's inability to meet debt obligations.

methods_unique.name: Simulation Analysis
methods_unique.description: Uses statistical distributions to model potential outcomes of investments.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Inputs must be based on analysis and data, not guesswork. Requires understanding of different probability distributions.

methods_unique.name: Risk-Adjusted Discount Rates
methods_unique.description: Using different discount rates for assets based on perceived risk.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Higher discount rate for riskier asset when valuing with simulations.

methods_unique.name: Simulation Redo Method
methods_unique.description: Redoing simulations using the risk-free rate as the discount rate to compare standard deviations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Caution needed if choosing based on standard deviation alone, as it may penalize diversifiable risk.

methods_unique.name: risk-adjusted discount rate
methods_unique.description: Adjusting discount rates for risks in valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Do not use risk-free rate to discount expected cash flows; instead, adjust the discount rate for different outcomes.

methods_unique.name: Probability-Weighted Expected Value
methods_unique.description: Estimating expected value considering probabilities of events.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Multiply the value of each outcome by its probability and sum to get expected value.

methods_unique.name: return_on_capital
methods_unique.description: Calculates return on capital using book values.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Equity + Debt - Cash) / Invested Capital

methods_unique.name: Contingent claim models
methods_unique.description: Valuing options like the option to delay investment, expand, or liquidate.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: NA

methods_unique.name: Choosing a Cash Flow Discounting Approach
methods_unique.description: Decide between firm and equity valuation models based on leverage stability.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: For stable leverage, use preferred model. For unstable leverage, use firm valuation for simplicity.

methods_unique.name: Dividend Discount Model vs Free Cash Flow to Equity
methods_unique.description: Choose DDM when precise cash flow estimation is difficult or restricted.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use DDM if unable to estimate cash flows precisely due to lack of information or control over management's actions.

methods_unique.name: Normalized Earnings Valuation
methods_unique.description: Value a firm using normalized earnings when negative or abnormal earnings are temporary.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Replace current earnings with normalized earnings if the causes for low earnings are transitory.

methods_unique.name: Adjusting Operating Margins
methods_unique.description: Improving operating margins over time to healthier levels.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value firm based on expected cash flows after margin improvement.

methods_unique.name: Capital Expenditure and Cash Flows
methods_unique.description: Infrastructure firms need high capital expenditure initially, leading to negative cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Future positive cash flows depend on reduction in capex and improved margins.

methods_unique.name: Valuing Start-ups
methods_unique.description: Assume high revenue growth and improving operating margins for young start-up companies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Value based on expected future cash flows from these assumptions.

methods_unique.name: Steady state models
methods_unique.description: Models that assume constant growth for stable-growth firms.

methods_unique.name: Two-stage discounted cash flow model
methods_unique.description: Used for moderate-growth firms to capture changes in firm characteristics over time.

methods_unique.name: Three-stage or n-stage models
methods_unique.description: Models used for high-growth-rate firms with longer transitions to stable growth.

methods_unique.name: Fundamentals approach
methods_unique.description: Use the variable most highly correlated with firm's value.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: For consumer product companies, price-earnings ratios are more suitable than for young technology companies.

methods_unique.name: Statistical approach
methods_unique.description: Run regressions of each multiple against fundamentals to determine R-squared.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: The multiple with the highest R-squared is best explained by fundamentals and should be used for valuation.

methods_unique.name: conventional multiple approach
methods_unique.description: Uses a specific multiple that is most widely used in a sector over time.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use the multiple with the highest R-squared to value companies in the sector.

methods_unique.name: Option Pricing Models
methods_unique.description: Models that value options based on underlying asset price, volatility, time to expiration, etc.

methods_unique.name: Financial Analysts Forecasts of Earnings
methods_unique.description: A method to predict earnings expectations.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Use financial analysts' forecasts as a proxy for market expectations.

methods_unique.name: After-tax cash flow (ATCF)
methods_unique.description: A method to value a company by considering after-tax cash flows.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Calculate ATCF = EBIT * (1 - tax rate) + Depreciation - Capital Expenditures

methods_unique.name: ARIMA models
methods_unique.description: Time series forecasting model
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average

methods_unique.name: Discounted terminal value
methods_unique.description: Valuation method for young/start-up companies.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Uses a discount rate to estimate future cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Distress-adjusted discounted cash flow (DCF) valuation
methods_unique.description: Adjusts DCF models for distressed firms.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Incorporates distress risk into the discount rate and terminal value.

methods_unique.name: F model
methods_unique.description: A valuation method.

methods_unique.name: Earnings Forecaster (Standard & Poor's)
methods_unique.description: Tool for forecasting earnings.

methods_unique.name: Enterprise value to EBITDA multiples
methods_unique.description: Ratio used in valuation analysis.

methods_unique.name: PE ratio
methods_unique.description: Price-to-Earnings ratio.

methods_unique.name: Discounted cash flow
methods_unique.description: Method for valuing a project, company or asset based on its expected future cash flows.

methods_unique.name: Efficient markets hypothesis
methods_unique.description: Theory that financial markets reflect all available information in asset prices.

methods_unique.name: E model (three-stage FCFE model)
methods_unique.description: A three-stage Free Cash Flow to Equity valuation model.

methods_unique.name: Expected growth. price-earnings (PE) ratio
methods_unique.description: Calculates the PE ratio based on expected extraordinary growth.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PE = Expected Earnings / Current Stock Price

methods_unique.name: economic value added
methods_unique.description: Measures a company’s economic profit.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: NOPAT - (WACC * Capital Invested)

methods_unique.name: historical market beta
methods_unique.description: A measure of a stock's volatility relative to the overall market based on historical data.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Covariance(Stock Returns, Market Returns) / Variance(Market Returns)

methods_unique.name: historical risk premiums
methods_unique.description: The difference between expected return and risk-free rate over time.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (Expected Return - Risk-Free Rate)

methods_unique.name: implied equity premium
methods_unique.description: Estimates the additional return investors expect for taking on extra risk in equities.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Historical Returns - Risk-Free Rate

methods_unique.name: internal rate of return (IRR)
methods_unique.description: The discount rate that makes the net present value of all cash flows from a particular project equal to zero.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: (NPV = 0) => IRR

methods_unique.name: Log-linear regression models
methods_unique.description: A statistical method for modeling relationships between variables.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Y = a + bX

methods_unique.name: Modified interest coverage ratio
methods_unique.description: A financial metric used to assess a company's ability to pay its debt obligations.

methods_unique.name: Modified multiples
methods_unique.description: Adjusted valuation multiples that account for specific firm characteristics or market conditions.

methods_unique.name: DCF valuation model
methods_unique.description: Used for valuing office buildings, NYSE Composite.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Present value of future cash flows discounted at the appropriate rate.

methods_unique.name: Net present value (NPV)
methods_unique.description: Estimation method for future projects.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Sum of the present values of all expected future cash inflows and outflows.

methods_unique.name: Ordinary least squares (OLS) regression
methods_unique.description: Statistical method for estimating the relationship between variables.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Linear regression analysis

methods_unique.name: Price-to-sales (PS) ratios
methods_unique.description: Used for high-growth firm valuation.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: PS = Market Value of Equity / Total Sales

methods_unique.name: Quick ratios
methods_unique.description: Measures short-term liquidity.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Current Assets - Inventory / Current Liabilities

methods_unique.name: Price-to-earnings ratio
methods_unique.description: Evaluates the relative value of a stock based on its earnings.
methods_unique.rules_or_formula: Market Price per Share / Earnings Per Share

methods_unique.name: Retention ratio
methods_unique.description: Calculation following firms in process of.
